1	\chapter{{\v C}ech computation of higher direct images $R^i f_*$ (unconditional)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean
4	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean
5	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/PresheafCech.lean
6	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FreePresheafComplex.lean
7	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechBridge.lean
8	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean
9	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
10	% quasi-coherent sheaves and \S Quasi-coherence of higher direct images
11	% (Lemmas \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial},
12	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero},
13	% \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent},
14	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}) (read from
15	% references/stacks-coherent.tex).
16	
17	\section{Setup and motivation}
18	
19	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
20	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
21	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
22	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
23	derived functor via injective resolutions, which requires the ambient category of
24	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives. The {\v C}ech approach
25	developed here sidesteps this requirement:
26	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
27	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
28	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
29	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
30	
31	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
32	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
33	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
34	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
35	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
36	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
37	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
38	\[
39	  \begin{array}{ccc}
40	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
41	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
42	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
43	  \end{array}
44	\]
45	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
46	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
47	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
48	
49	\section{The {\v C}ech nerve and {\v C}ech complex of an affine cover}
50	
51	\begin{definition}
52	
53	\leanok
54	[{\v C}ech nerve of an affine open cover]
55	  \label{def:cech_nerve}
56	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve}
57	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor, def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
58	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
59	  % quasi-coherent sheaves, opening paragraph and Lemma
60	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
61	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L36--67).
62	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
63	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an affine open.
64	  % Recall that a {\it standard open covering} of $U$ is a covering
65	  % of the form $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$
66	  % where $f_1, \ldots, f_n \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)$ generate
67	  % the unit ideal, see
68	  % Schemes, Definition \ref{schemes-definition-standard-covering}."
69	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
70	  quasi-coherent sheaves.}
71	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
72	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite open cover of \(X\) by
73	  affine opens, with index set \(I\). For a finite nonempty tuple
74	  \(i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I\) write
75	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = U_{i_0} \cap \cdots \cap U_{i_p}\) for the corresponding
76	  intersection. The \emph{{\v C}ech nerve} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the augmented
77	  \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) whose object in
78	  cosimplicial degree \(p\) is the direct image, along the open immersions
79	  \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\), of the restriction
80	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (or of any chosen quasi-coherent sheaf) to the
81	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
82	  \[
83	    [p] \;\longmapsto\;
84	      \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
85	        (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_* \,\bigl(\,\cdot\,|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
86	  \]
87	  with cofaces given by the restriction maps that omit one index and
88	  codegeneracies by the maps that repeat one index; the augmentation in degree
89	  \(-1\) is the object on all of \(X\). The variance is essential: the geometric
90	  nerve of the cover (the iterated fibre powers of \(\coprod_i U_i\) over \(X\),
91	  introduced in \S\ref{sec:cech_three_part} below) is a \emph{simplicial} object
92	  in \emph{schemes}, but applying the covariant direct-image functor turns it into
93	  a \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); consequently the
94	  associated alternating-coface complex is a \emph{cochain} complex (differentials
95	  raising degree), not a chain complex. When \(X\) is separated each intersection
96	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is itself affine. The local affine model is the
97	  \emph{standard open covering} \(U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) of an affine open
98	  \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) by basic opens, where \(f_1, \ldots, f_n \in A\)
99	  generate the unit ideal; on such a cover the intersections are again basic opens
100	  \(D(f_{i_0} \cdots f_{i_p})\).
101	\end{definition}
102	
103	\begin{definition}
104	
105	\leanok
106	[{\v C}ech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf]
107	  \label{def:cech_complex}
108	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex}
109	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, def:relative_cech_complex_of_nerve}
110	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
111	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof)
112	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L59--77).
113	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
114	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
115	  % is identified with the complex
116	  % $$
117	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
118	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
119	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
120	  % \ldots
121	  % $$
122	  % We are asked to show that the extended complex
123	  % \begin{equation}
124	  % \label{equation-extended}
125	  % 0 \to
126	  % M \to
127	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
128	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
129	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
130	  % \ldots
131	  % \end{equation}
132	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
133	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact."
134	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
135	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover {\v C}ech
136	  vanishing).}
137	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
138	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with
139	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) affine (e.g.\ \(X\) separated). The
140	  \emph{{\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
141	  is the cochain complex obtained by applying the global-sections functor to the
142	  {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\);
143	  in degree \(p\) it is
144	  \[
145	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
146	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
147	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
148	  \]
149	  with differential \(d : \check{\mathcal{C}}^p \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^{p+1}\) the
150	  alternating sum \((d s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
151	  s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}\) of the
152	  restriction maps. Over an affine \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) with
153	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) and a standard cover by the
154	  \(D(f_i)\), this is the complex
155	  \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to \cdots\) of
156	  localisations. The \emph{relative {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet
157	  (\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) for \(f : X \to S\) is the analogous complex in
158	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) obtained by applying \(f_*\) over each finite
159	  intersection instead of global sections; its terms are
160	  \(\prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*
161	  \bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr)\), and it is a complex in
162	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) because each \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine and the
163	  pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated
164	  morphism is quasi-coherent.
165	\end{definition}
166	
167	\subsection{The three-part construction of the relative {\v C}ech complex}
168	\label{sec:cech_three_part}
169	
170	It is convenient to factor the construction of
171	Definitions~\ref{def:cech_nerve}--\ref{def:cech_complex} into three independent
172	pieces. Two of them are purely formal and unconditional; the sole non-trivial
173	mathematical content is isolated in the middle piece.
174	
175	\paragraph{(1) Geometric backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow.}
176	Package the affine cover as a single morphism
177	\(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) (the canonical map out of the
178	coproduct induced by the inclusions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\)). The augmented
179	{\v C}ech nerve of this arrow is an augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
180	\(X\): its object in simplicial degree \(p\) is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
181	\[
182	  \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
183	    \;=\;
184	    \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
185	      U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
186	\]
187	with the augmentation the cover map to \(X\). Because the \(U_i\) are open
188	subschemes of \(X\), each fibre product \(U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p}\)
189	is canonically the intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). This backbone exists
190	\emph{unconditionally}: it uses only that the category of schemes has coproducts
191	and finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the {\v C}ech nerve of an
192	arrow). It carries no sheaf-theoretic data yet — it is the simplicial diagram of
193	spaces over which the construction is indexed.
194	
195	\paragraph{(2) Push--pull functor — lifting the backbone to modules.}
196	The geometric backbone is turned into a cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module by
197	the \emph{relative-direct-image functor on the over-category}
198	\[
199	  G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
200	  \qquad
201	  (Y \xrightarrow{\,p\,} X) \;\longmapsto\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
202	\]
203	sending an \(X\)-scheme \(p : Y \to X\) to the pushforward along \(p\) of the
204	pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\). Composing \(G\) with the (opposite of the) simplicial
205	backbone of (1) produces exactly the augmented cosimplicial object of
206	Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: on the degree-\(p\) piece
207	\(p : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\) one has
208	\(p_* p^*\mathcal{F} = (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\),
209	and a morphism of \(X\)-schemes \(h : (Y_1, p_1) \to (Y_2, p_2)\) (so
210	\(h \circ p_2 = p_1\)\,, reading \(p\) as the structure map) induces the
211	restriction comparison \(G(p_2) \to G(p_1)\) via the unit/counit of the
212	pull-push adjunction together with the over-triangle identifying the two
213	structure maps. This functor \(G\) is the \emph{one non-formal step} in the whole
214	construction. Its functoriality --- that \(G\) respects identities and composition
215	of morphisms over \(X\) --- is precisely a statement about the coherence of the
216	comparison isomorphisms \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) and
217	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\) for pushforward and pullback of sheaves of
218	modules, together with their compatibility with the adjunction units. Concretely,
219	pullback and pushforward of sheaves of modules are organised as a
220	pseudofunctor \(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to
221	\mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\) (sending a morphism to the adjoint pair
222	\(p^* \dashv p_*\)), and the comparison isomorphisms
223	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\), \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) are its structure
224	\(2\)-cells. The functoriality of \(G\) needs exactly three facts: the mate identity
225	expressing the conjugate of the inverse pullback comparison as the pushforward
226	comparison, its normalisation at the identity morphism, and the pseudofunctor
227	unitor and pentagon (associativity) coherences. The laws are nonetheless not formal
228	trivialities: each is a multi-step mate calculation that transports the comparison
229	isomorphisms along the over-triangle \(g \circ p_2 = p_1\) of structure maps and
230	past the adjunction unit, transporting along the over-triangle equality; the
231	obstacle is the bookkeeping of those transports, not a missing geometric ingredient.
232	
233	\paragraph{(3) Coherence-free plumbing — from the nerve to the cochain complex.}
234	The passage from the augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module of (1)--(2)
235	to the relative {\v C}ech complex in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) is again purely
236	formal and uses \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence. One forgets the
237	augmentation, pushes the cosimplicial object forward along \(f : X \to S\) (a
238	covariant operation applied degreewise), and takes the alternating-coface-map
239	cochain complex of the resulting cosimplicial object of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules.
240	This step relies only on the preadditivity of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\): the
241	alternating sum \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j d^j\) of the cofaces is defined in any
242	preadditive category and squares to zero by the cosimplicial identities. Thus the
243	relative {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
244	of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} is genuinely \emph{defined} in terms of the
245	{\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: once the nerve is constructed,
246	the complex follows with no further input. In summary, of the three pieces only
247	the push--pull functor (2) requires content beyond formal category theory, and
248	that content is the pseudofunctor coherence of pushforward and pullback: the
249	comparison \(2\)-cells of the pseudofunctor
250	\(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to \mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\)
251	and their unitor/pentagon identities.
252	
253	\paragraph{Formal bricks of the three-part construction.}
254	The geometric backbone (1), the push--pull functor (2), and the plumbing (3) are
255	recorded as the following declarations. The backbone and plumbing are
256	unconditional; the push--pull object and morphism maps are likewise unconditional
257	bricks, and the functor laws assembling them are the lone coherence statement.
258	
259	\begin{definition}\leanok
260	[Arrow of an affine cover]
261	  \label{def:cover_arrow}
262	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrow}
263	  Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be an affine open cover. Its
264	  \emph{cover arrow} is the single morphism
265	  \(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) obtained as the universal map out of
266	  the coproduct induced by the open immersions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\). Packaging
267	  the cover as one arrow lets the generic {\v C}ech-nerve-of-an-arrow machinery
268	  apply.
269	\end{definition}
270	
271	\begin{definition}[Augmented {\v C}ech nerve of a cover]
272	  \label{def:cover_cech_nerve}
273	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerve, AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOver, AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOverAug}
274	  \uses{def:cover_arrow}
275	  The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of the cover arrow of
276	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_arrow}: the augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
277	  \(X\) whose degree-\(p\) object is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
278	  \[
279	    \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
280	      = \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
281	        U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
282	  \]
283	  with augmentation the cover map to \(X\). It exists unconditionally because the
284	  category of schemes has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the
285	  {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow). This is the geometric backbone (1).
286	\end{definition}
287	
288	\begin{definition}\leanok
289	[Push--pull object map]
290	  \label{def:push_pull_obj}
291	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullObj}
292	  The object map of the push--pull functor \(G\): to an \(X\)-scheme
293	  \(p : Y \to X\) it assigns
294	  \[
295	    G(Y, p) \;=\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
296	  \]
297	  the pushforward along \(p\) of the pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\).
298	\end{definition}
299	
300	\begin{definition}[Push--pull comparison map]
301	  \label{def:push_pull_map}
302	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_eq_raw, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_self, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_self_gen, AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardComp_hom_app_id, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_comp}
303	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj}
304	  The morphism map of \(G\). A morphism \(g : (Y_2, p_2) \to (Y_1, p_1)\) of
305	  \(X\)-schemes has underlying map \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) satisfying the
306	  over-triangle \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\); the contravariant functor produces the
307	  restriction comparison \(G(Y_1, p_1) \to G(Y_2, p_2)\) as the five-step composite
308	  \[
309	    p_{1*}\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
310	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{1*}(\eta)\,}
311	    p_{1*}\,\bar g_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
312	      \xrightarrow{\,\sim\,}
313	    (p_1 \circ \bar g)_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
314	      \;=\;
315	    p_{2*}\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
316	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{2*}(\sim)\,}
317	    p_{2*}\,p_2^*\mathcal{F},
318	  \]
319	  where \(\eta\) is the unit of the adjunction \(\bar g^* \dashv \bar g_*\), the
320	  second arrow is the pushforward comparison \(p_{1*}\bar g_* \cong (p_1 \bar g)_*\),
321	  the equality is the substitution \(p_2 = p_1 \circ \bar g\) of the over-triangle,
322	  and the last arrow applies \(p_{2*}\) to the pullback comparison
323	  \(\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (p_1 \bar g)^* = p_2^*\). The two over-triangle
324	  substitutions are realised as coercions along \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\).
325	\end{definition}
326	
327	\begin{lemma}
328	[Push--pull functor — identity law]
329	  \label{lem:push_pull_id}
330	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_id}
331	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map}
332	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} respects identities:
333	  \(G(\mathrm{id}_Y) = \mathrm{id}_{G(Y)}\) for every \(X\)-scheme \(Y\).
334	\end{lemma}
335	
336	\begin{proof}
337	  A direct calculation with comparison isomorphisms, with no sectionwise content.
338	  Rewrite the head \(p_*(\eta)\) followed by the pushforward comparison as the
339	  conjugate-mate of the inverse pullback comparison; the identity leg then collapses
340	  by the identity normalisation of the pseudofunctor together with right unitality,
341	  and the two over-triangle transport coercions cancel against the unitality coercion.
342	\end{proof}
343	
344	\begin{lemma}
345	[Push--pull functor — composition law]
346	  \label{lem:push_pull_comp}
347	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_pentagon}
348	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map, lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
349	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} is contravariantly functorial:
350	  for composable morphisms \(h : Y_3 \to Y_2\), \(g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) of \(X\)-schemes,
351	  \[
352	    G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g).
353	  \]
354	  Together with Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} this assembles
355	  Definitions~\ref{def:push_pull_obj}--\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a functor
356	  \(G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \to \operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); composing \(G\) with
357	  the backbone of Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} yields the {\v C}ech nerve of
358	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}.
359	\end{lemma}
360	
361	\begin{proof}
362	  \uses{lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
363	  The composition identity \(G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g)\) follows from three
364	  coherence facts. First, the composite adjunction unit \(\eta^{g \circ h}\) decomposes into
365	  the iterated units \(\eta^{g}\), \(\eta^{h}\) via the mate identity of
366	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_unit_mate} and the naturality of the adjunction unit. Second,
367	  the resulting pullback-side identity is the \emph{pentagon coherence} of the pullback
368	  pseudofunctor — the associativity \(2\)-cell relating the two bracketings of the comparison
369	  isomorphism \((p \circ q \circ r)^* \cong r^* q^* p^*\). Third, the over-triangle transport
370	  coherences are absorbed by Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}.
371	\end{proof}
372	
373	\begin{lemma}
374	[Base-change unit (mate) identity for the push--pull head]
375	  \label{lem:push_pull_unit_mate}
376	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_mate}
377	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
378	  For composable scheme morphisms \(f : A \to B\), \(p : B \to Z\) and a
379	  \(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-module \(N\), the adjunction unit \(\eta^p_N\) of
380	  \(p^* \dashv p_*\) at \(N\), followed by \(p_*\) of the unit \(\eta^f\) for
381	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) at \(p^*N\), followed by the pushforward comparison
382	  \(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\), equals the unit \(\eta^{f \circ p}_N\) for the
383	  composite followed by \((f \circ p)_*\) applied to the inverse pullback
384	  comparison \((f \circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\):
385	  \[
386	    \eta^p_N \;\circ\; p_*(\eta^f_{p^*N}) \;\circ\;
387	      \bigl(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\bigr)
388	    \;=\;
389	    \eta^{f \circ p}_N \;\circ\; (f \circ p)_*\bigl((f\circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\bigr)^{-1}.
390	  \]
391	  This is the mate-calculus core that converts the single-morphism unit
392	  \(\eta^{f \circ p}\) into the iterated units \(\eta^p, \eta^f\); it is the
393	  reusable ingredient consumed by the functoriality (pentagon) law of the
394	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map}.
395	\end{lemma}
396	
397	\begin{proof}
398	  A direct mate-calculus computation: unfold both sides using the definition of
399	  the adjunction unit for the composite and the pseudofunctor structure maps, then
400	  verify the equality by the mate identity for the pushforward/pullback comparison.
401	\end{proof}
402	
403	\begin{lemma}
404	[Over-triangle transport cancellation for the push--pull tail]
405	  \label{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
406	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_transport_cancel}
407	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
408	  Let \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\), \(p_1 : Y_1 \to X\), \(p_2 : Y_2 \to X\) satisfy the
409	  over-triangle \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\), and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a
410	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then the pullback-comparison leg of the push--pull map,
411	  conjugated by the two \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions along the over-triangle,
412	  collapses to the transport-light form
413	  \[
414	    e_1 \;\circ\; p_{2*}\bigl((\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (\bar g p_1)^*)_{\mathcal{F}}\bigr)
415	      \;\circ\; e_2
416	    \;=\;
417	    (\bar g p_1)_*\bigl((\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (\bar g p_1)^*)_{\mathcal{F}}\bigr)
418	      \;\circ\; e_3,
419	  \]
420	  where \(e_1, e_2, e_3\) are the \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions induced by
421	  \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\). It states the over-triangle cancellation in the
422	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} with the
423	  over-triangle equality as a free hypothesis, so a single substitution turns the
424	  transports into identities; it is the reusable pre-coherence brick for the
425	  composition (pentagon) law.
426	\end{lemma}
427	
428	\begin{proof}
429	  Substitute the over-triangle equality \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\) to replace
430	  transport coercions by identities, then verify the cancellation by direct
431	  calculation with the pushforward comparison.
432	\end{proof}
433	
434	\begin{definition}
435	[Push--pull functor]
436	  \label{def:push_pull_functor}
437	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullFunctor}
438	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
439	  The \emph{push--pull functor}
440	  \[
441	    G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
442	  \]
443	  assembling the object map \(p \mapsto p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F}\) of
444	  Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_obj} and the comparison morphism map of
445	  Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a genuine functor. Functoriality is exactly the two
446	  laws established above: the identity law
447	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} and the composition law Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_comp}. The
448	  functor is contravariant in the over-category \((X/\mathbf{Sch})\); it is the lift of the
449	  geometric backbone (1) to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described as step (2) above.
450	\end{definition}
451	
452	\begin{definition}
453	[{\v C}ech nerve cosimplicial object]
454	  \label{def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial}
455	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechNerveCosimplicial}
456	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor}
457	  The (non-augmented) cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module obtained by transporting the
458	  geometric {\v C}ech backbone in \((X/\mathbf{Sch})\) of
459	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} through the push--pull functor
460	  \(G\) of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_functor}: degreewise, the \(p\)-th term is the
461	  product over \((i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}\) of the \((p+1)\)-fold-intersection
462	  pushforwards \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\). This is the
463	  underlying cosimplicial object that the {\v C}ech nerve of
464	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} augments by the structure map \(\mathcal{F} \to
465	  \mathcal{C}^0\).
466	\end{definition}
467	
468	\begin{definition}\leanok
469	[Relative {\v C}ech complex from a nerve]
470	  \label{def:relative_cech_complex_of_nerve}
471	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.relativeCechComplexOfNerve}
472	  \uses{def:cech_nerve}
473	  Given \(f : X \to S\) and an augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
474	  \(N\), the \emph{relative {\v C}ech cochain complex} in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\)
475	  obtained by forgetting the augmentation of \(N\), pushing the resulting
476	  cosimplicial object forward degreewise along \(f\), and taking the
477	  alternating-coface-map cochain complex. This passage uses only the preadditivity
478	  of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) and \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence; it is
479	  the coherence-free plumbing (3) that turns the nerve of
480	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} into the {\v C}ech complex.
481	\end{definition}

483	\section{Affine acyclicity of the {\v C}ech complex}
484	
485	\begin{definition}
486	[Standard affine cover from a spanning family]
487	  \label{def:standard_affine_cover}
488	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}
489	  \mathlibok
490	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open}).}
491	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring (so \(R = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R,
492	  \mathcal{O})\)) and let \(s : \iota \to R\) be a family of elements that generate
493	  the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\). The
494	  \emph{standard affine open cover} of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) associated with
495	  \(s\) is the affine open cover whose index set is \(\iota\), whose \(i\)-th member
496	  is the basic open \(D(s_i)\), and whose \(i\)-th structure morphism is the map of
497	  affine schemes induced by the localisation homomorphism \(R \to R_{s_i}\); that
498	  is, in Mathlib notation the defining equation
499	  \(\bigl(\text{affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}\bigr)_f\, i =
500	  \operatorname{Spec.map}\bigl(\text{algebraMap}\; R\; (\operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i))\bigr)\),
501	  so that the sections over the \(i\)-th piece are the \emph{away} localisation
502	  \(R_{s_i} = \operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i)\) and the geometric piece is
503	  \(D(s_i)\). This is exactly the standard open covering
504	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_i D(s_i)\) by basic opens, packaged as a single
505	  cover object. It is the spanning-family bundle \((s, hs)\) that the affine
506	  {\v C}ech vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is stated over.
507	\end{definition}
508	
509	\subsection{The categorical--module bridge (L1)}
510	\label{sec:cech_l1_bridge}
511	
512	The affine vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is a statement about the
513	\emph{section} {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
514	\mathcal{F})\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} --- a cochain complex of
515	abelian groups --- whereas the combinatorial contracting-homotopy core operates on a
516	concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules. The passage between the two, the
517	\emph{categorical--module bridge}, decomposes into two self-contained statements: the
518	section identification (each section group is an away localisation, with the
519	restriction maps the canonical localisation maps) and the homology-to-exactness
520	reduction (vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
521	underlying localised-module complex). These are isolated as
522	Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and
523	Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} below; the assembly --- feeding each
524	positive-degree node through the local-to-global exactness criterion and closing with
525	the dependent combinatorial port --- is carried out in the proof of
526	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
527	
528	\begin{definition}
529	
530	[Quasi-coherent sections over a basic open are an away localisation]
531	  \label{def:qcoh_sections_localized}
532	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcohSectionsAwayLocalized,
533	    AlgebraicGeometry.basicOpen_sprod,
534	    AlgebraicGeometry.iInf_fin_succ,
535	    AlgebraicGeometry.qcohRestriction_eq_comparison,
536	    AlgebraicGeometry.tP,
537	    AlgebraicGeometry.tU,
538	    AlgebraicGeometry.phiL,
539	    AlgebraicGeometry.phi,
540	    AlgebraicGeometry.phi_eq_phiL,
541	    AlgebraicGeometry.restr_bridge}
542	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:standard_affine_cover}
543	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
544	  % items (4)--(5) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L701--707).
545	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "(4) For every $f\in R$ we have $\Gamma(D(f), \widetilde M) = M_f$
546	  % as an $R_f$-module.
547	  % (5) Whenever $D(g) \subset D(f)$ the restriction mappings
548	  % on $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$ and $\widetilde M$
549	  % are the maps
550	  % $R_f \to R_g$ and $M_f \to M_g$ from Lemma
551	  % \ref{lemma-standard-open}."
552	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
553	  items (4)--(5).}
554	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
555	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module, and write
556	  \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global
557	  sections. For every \(g \in R\), the restriction-of-sections map
558	  \(M = \mathcal{F}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g))\) exhibits the
559	  section group \(\mathcal{F}(D(g))\) as the away localisation \(M_g\) of \(M\); that
560	  is, this map is an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for the multiplicative
561	  submonoid \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g\) of powers of \(g\). Moreover, for
562	  \(D(g_2) \subseteq D(g_1)\) the restriction map
563	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(g_1)) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g_2))\) is, under these identifications, the
564	  canonical localisation map \(M_{g_1} \to M_{g_2}\) (the unique \(R\)-linear map
565	  compatible with the localisation units). Item~(4) of Tag~01HV gives the first
566	  assertion for \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) (\(\Gamma(D(g), \widetilde{M}) = M_g\)
567	  as an \(R_g\)-module) and item~(5) gives the second (the restriction along
568	  \(D(g) \subset D(f)\) is the localisation map \(M_f \to M_g\)).
569	
570	  The module-level input on the side of Mathlib is the development
571	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}: the comparison map
572	  \((\operatorname{tilde.toOpen} M\, (D(g))).\mathrm{hom}\) carries the instance
573	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g)\), and
574	  \(\operatorname{isUnit\_algebraMap\_end\_basicOpen}\) records that \(g\) acts
575	  invertibly on the sections over \(D(g)\); the restriction compatibility is
576	  \(\operatorname{tilde.toOpen\_res}\). These supply the statement \emph{verbatim} for
577	  the standard sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\). For an \emph{arbitrary} quasi-coherent
578	  \(\mathcal{F}\) the remaining step is the canonical comparison
579	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})}\), i.e.\
580	  that the counit \(\operatorname{Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism. This
581	  is \emph{not} yet available in Mathlib for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\):
582	  Mathlib provides it only from a \emph{global} presentation
583	  (\(\operatorname{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\)), whereas quasi-coherence
584	  supplies only \emph{local} presentation data on a cover. Globalising the local data
585	  on the affine \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) --- the affine equivalence
586	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\), Stacks
587	  Tag~01I8 --- is the one genuine gap this definition records as a build target; it is
588	  the canonical missing affine equivalence and is self-contained.
589	
590	  Two combinatorial--geometric ingredients package the identification into the form
591	  consumed by Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}. First, the multi-index
592	  intersection identity
593	  \(\bigl(\bigcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)})\bigr) = D\bigl(\prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\bigr)\) in
594	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R).\mathrm{Opens}\) (\(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\), proved
595	  by induction on the index length using the infimum-splitting helper
596	  \(\operatorname{iInf\_fin\_succ}\) and \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen\_mul}\)):
597	  it rewrites the \((p+1)\)-fold intersection over a multi-index \(\sigma\) as the single
598	  basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\), so the
599	  section group there is the away localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). Second, the restriction
600	  compatibility of item~(5) is made explicit: for a basic-open inclusion
601	  \(D(b) \subseteq D(a)\) the presheaf restriction map of \(\widetilde{M}\) between the
602	  two section groups \emph{is} the away-localisation comparison
603	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison}\) of the two localisation units
604	  (\(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\), by the uniqueness
605	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison\_unique}\) of a map of localised modules);
606	  this is the single differential brick that the {\v C}ech coface match of
607	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} feeds on.
608	
609	  The identification is packaged by a few helpers: the two localised-module endpoints
610	  \(\operatorname{tP}, \operatorname{tU}\) (the section groups
611	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) named as away localisations at the two indices
612	  involved in a face), the per-\(\sigma\) comparison
613	  \(\varphi_\sigma = \operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.iso}\) realised both as the bare
614	  localisation comparison \(\operatorname{phiL}\) and as its sheaf-level avatar
615	  \(\operatorname{phi}\) (their agreement is \(\operatorname{phi\_eq\_phiL}\)), and the
616	  accessor-bridge \(\operatorname{restr\_bridge}\) that exhibits the presheaf
617	  restriction map through these comparisons.
618	\end{definition}
619	
620	\begin{lemma}
621	[Section {\v C}ech homology vanishes iff the localised complex is exact]
622	  \label{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}
623	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_homology_exact,
624	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts,
625	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.isUnit_powers,
626	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.mul,
627	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd,
628	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison,
629	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_apply,
630	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp,
631	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply,
632	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_structure,
633	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_self,
634	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_unique,
635	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoeff,
636	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison,
637	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoface,
638	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact,
639	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechPrepend,
640	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hcomm,
641	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hsh,
642	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hu,
643	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod,
644	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod_cons,
645	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod_succAbove_dvd}
646	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized, lem:section_cech_module_exact,
647	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
648	    lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
649	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
650	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (the {\v C}ech complex is
651	  % the complex of localisations) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
652	  % L58--67).
653	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
654	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
655	  % is identified with the complex
656	  % $$
657	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
658	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
659	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
660	  % \ldots
661	  % $$"
662	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
663	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (identification with the
664	  complex of localisations).}
665	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
666	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family
667	  \(s : \iota \to R\). Under the section identification of
668	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, the section {\v C}ech complex
669	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
670	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
671	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
672	  \[
673	    D^\bullet : \quad
674	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
675	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
676	    \cdots,
677	    \qquad
678	    s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
679	  \]
680	  whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. The
681	  intended top-level conclusion is the directed homology vanishing
682	  \[
683	    1 \leq p
684	    \;\Longrightarrow\;
685	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
686	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr),
687	  \]
688	  which is the form the affine vanishing
689	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (\(\operatorname{sectionCech\_affine\_vanishing}\))
690	  consumes; it is read off from positive-degree exactness of the underlying group
691	  homomorphisms,
692	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr)\), which is
693	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
694	
695	  The reduction from the abstract homology to this group-level exactness is \emph{not}
696	  a \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\) statement: the degree-\(p\) object of
697	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the \emph{categorical
698	  product}
699	  \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}
700	  \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) in the category \(\mathrm{Ab}\) of abelian groups (the
701	  alternating-coface-map complex of the \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued cosimplicial object
702	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\)), not an \(R\)-module object, so the
703	  module-category criterion \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) does
704	  not apply. The correct chain runs through three steps, isolated as the sub-lemmas
705	  below: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at
706	  \(p\) (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)); \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
707	  of a short complex in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) is the underlying-group exactness
708	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\) via the abelian
709	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
710	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}); and the categorical product is moved to the
711	  per-\(\sigma\) localised modules by the element-level product equivalence
712	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
713	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\)
714	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), under which the {\v C}ech coface
715	  differential matches the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
716	  \(D^\bullet\) (the abstract cosimplicial unfold
717	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} followed by the tilde-bridge identification
718	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). Transporting the
719	  already-proved exactness \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
720	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the product equivalence then
721	  discharges the group-level exactness. The active target is the tilde case
722	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\), for which the per-coordinate comparisons are
723	  immediate from \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\); a general
724	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is reduced to this case via the affine equivalence
725	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag~01I8), the
726	  separately-deferred globalisation gap recorded in
727	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
728	\end{lemma}
729	
730	\begin{proof}
731	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
732	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
733	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
734	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
735	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
736	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
737	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
738	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
739	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
740	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
741	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
742	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
743	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) (the coface match of
744	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). The homology vanishing then follows in
745	  three steps. First, \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) turns
746	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology at \(p\) into \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
747	  the short complex at \(p\). Second, because the degree-\(p\) objects are
748	  \emph{categorical products in \(\mathrm{Ab}\)} rather than \(R\)-modules, the abelian
749	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
750	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}) --- not the module-category criterion ---
751	  reduces \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) to the underlying-group exactness
752	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\). Third, the
753	  element-level product equivalence
754	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
755	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
756	  identifies each product group with the per-\(\sigma\) localised module \(D^p\), so the
757	  group-level exactness is exactly \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to

2660	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_complex_mapop_iso}) identifies it with the augmentation
2661	  of the section {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
2662	  \mathcal{I})\) onto the mapped image of the degree-\(0\)-concentrated target
2663	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\). The latter has vanishing homology in positive
2664	  degrees, so the section {\v C}ech complex does too. Hence
2665	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and
2666	  \(\check{H}^0(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = \mathcal{I}(U)\). This direct route uses
2667	  neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
2668	  \(\delta\)-functor identification.
2669	\end{proof}
2670	
2671	\begin{lemma}
2672	[Surjectivity on sections from cofinal {\v C}ech-$H^1$ vanishing]
2673	  \label{lem:ses_cech_h1}
2674	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ses_cech_h1}
2675	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
2676	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-ses-cech-h1}
2677	  % (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1592--1628).
2678	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a ringed space.
2679	  % Let
2680	  % $$
2681	  % 0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H} \to 0
2682	  % $$
2683	  % be a short exact sequence of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.
2684	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an open subset.
2685	  % If there exists a cofinal system of open coverings $\mathcal{U}$
2686	  % of $U$ such that $\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$,
2687	  % then the map $\mathcal{G}(U) \to \mathcal{H}(U)$ is
2688	  % surjective."
2689	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
2690	  \texttt{lemma-ses-cech-h1}.}
2691	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and let
2692	  \[
2693	    0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H} \to 0
2694	  \]
2695	  be a short exact sequence of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Let \(U \subset X\) be
2696	  open. If there is a cofinal system of open coverings \(\mathcal{U}\) of \(U\) with
2697	  \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\), then the map on sections
2698	  \(\mathcal{G}(U) \to \mathcal{H}(U)\) is surjective. Equivalently, the short exact
2699	  sequence is exact on sections over such \(U\).
2700	\end{lemma}
2701	
2702	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Take an element $s \in \mathcal{H}(U)$. Choose an open
2703	% covering
2704	% $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ such that
2705	% (a) $\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ and (b)
2706	% $s|_{U_i}$ is the image of a section $s_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)$.
2707	% Since we can certainly find $\mathcal{U}$ such that (b) holds
2708	% it follows from the assumptions of the lemma that we can find
2709	% $\mathcal{U}$ such that (a) and (b) both hold.
2710	% Consider the sections
2711	% $$
2712	% s_{i_0i_1} = s_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0i_1}} - s_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0i_1}}.
2713	% $$
2714	% Since $s_i$ lifts $s$ we see that $s_{i_0i_1} \in \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0i_1})$.
2715	% By the vanishing of $\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$ we can
2716	% find sections $t_i \in \mathcal{F}(U_i)$ such that
2717	% $$
2718	% s_{i_0i_1} = t_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0i_1}} - t_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0i_1}}.
2719	% $$
2720	% Then clearly the sections $s_i - t_i$ satisfy the sheaf condition
2721	% and glue to a section of $\mathcal{G}$ over $U$ which maps to $s$.
2722	% Hence we win."
2723	\begin{proof}
2724	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
2725	  Let \(s \in \mathcal{H}(U)\). Choose an open covering \(\mathcal{U} : U =
2726	  \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) fine enough that (a) \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U},
2727	  \mathcal{F}) = 0\) and (b) each restriction \(s|_{U_i}\) lifts to a section
2728	  \(s_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)\); both can be arranged simultaneously because the
2729	  coverings with property (a) are cofinal and (b) holds on any sufficiently fine
2730	  cover (surjectivity of \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H}\) is a local condition).
2731	  On the double overlaps form
2732	  \[
2733	    s_{i_0 i_1} = s_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}} - s_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}}.
2734	  \]
2735	  Because the \(s_i\) all lift the single section \(s\), the difference
2736	  \(s_{i_0 i_1}\) maps to \(0\) in \(\mathcal{H}\), hence lies in the subsheaf
2737	  \(\mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 i_1})\); the family \((s_{i_0 i_1})\) is a {\v C}ech
2738	  \(1\)-cocycle for \(\mathcal{F}\). By \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\)
2739	  it is a coboundary: there are \(t_i \in \mathcal{F}(U_i)\) with
2740	  \(s_{i_0 i_1} = t_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}} - t_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}}\). Then the
2741	  corrected sections \(s_i - t_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)\) agree on overlaps, so by the
2742	  sheaf condition they glue to a global section of \(\mathcal{G}\) over \(U\) whose
2743	  image in \(\mathcal{H}\) is \(s\). Hence \(\mathcal{G}(U) \to \mathcal{H}(U)\) is
2744	  surjective.
2745	\end{proof}
2746	
2747	\begin{lemma}
2748	[Serre vanishing on affines]
2749	  \label{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}
2750	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affine_serre_vanishing}
2751	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}
2752	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG, Lemma
2753	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} (Serre vanishing on
2754	  % affines) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L145--155).
2755	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Serre vanishing: Higher cohomology vanishes on affine schemes
2756	  % for quasi-coherent modules.
2757	  % Let $X$ be a scheme.
2758	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
2759	  % For any affine open $U \subset X$ we have
2760	  % $H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for all $p > 0$."
2761	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG
2762	  (\texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}, Serre vanishing on
2763	  affines).}
2764	  Let \(U\) be an affine scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
2765	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module. Then the sheaf cohomology of \(\mathcal{F}\) vanishes in
2766	  all positive degrees:
2767	  \[
2768	    H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad \text{for all } p > 0.
2769	  \]
2770	\end{lemma}
2771	
2772	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We are going to apply
2773	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-vanish-basis}.
2774	% As our basis $\mathcal{B}$ for the topology of $X$ we are going to use
2775	% the affine opens of $X$.
2776	% As our set $\text{Cov}$ of open coverings we are going to use the standard
2777	% open coverings of affine opens of $X$.
2778	% Next we check that conditions (1), (2) and (3) of
2779	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-vanish-basis}
2780	% hold. Note that the intersection of standard opens in an affine is
2781	% another standard open. Hence property (1) holds.
2782	% The coverings form a cofinal system of open coverings of any element
2783	% of $\mathcal{B}$, see
2784	% Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-standard-open}.
2785	% Hence (2) holds.
2786	% Finally, condition (3) of the lemma follows from
2787	% Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}."
2788	\begin{proof}
2789	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}
2790	  Take the affine opens of \(U\) as a basis \(\mathcal{B}\) of the topology and the
2791	  standard open covers of affine opens as the admissible covers. The intersection of
2792	  two standard opens in an affine is again a standard open, so \(\mathcal{B}\) is
2793	  closed under the relevant intersections; and the standard covers form a cofinal
2794	  system among the open covers of any basic open. The standard-cover {\v C}ech
2795	  vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} supplies the acyclicity
2796	  hypothesis. The {\v C}ech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis
2797	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}) then identifies the sheaf cohomology
2798	  of \(\mathcal{F}\) on each basic open with its {\v C}ech cohomology for a standard
2799	  cover, whence \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\).
2800	\end{proof}
2801	
2802	\begin{lemma}
2803	[{\v C}ech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis]
2804	  \label{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}
2805	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis}
2806	  \uses{def:cech_complex, lem:injective_cech_acyclic, lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
2807	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01EO, Lemma
2808	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (a variant of \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish}; the
2809	  % basis-comparison criterion for {\v C}ech vs.\ sheaf cohomology), statement and
2810	  % proof (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1695--1776).
2811	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "(Variant of Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-vanish}.)
2812	  % Let $X$ be a ringed space.
2813	  % Let $\mathcal{B}$ be a basis for the topology on $X$.
2814	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
2815	  % Assume there exists a set of open coverings $\text{Cov}$
2816	  % with the following properties:
2817	  % \begin{enumerate}
2818	  % \item For every $\mathcal{U} \in \text{Cov}$
2819	  % with $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ we have

288	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech complex -/
289	
290	/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**
291	
292	For a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
293	the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
294	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — surjectivity on sections from {\v C}ech-`H¹`
vanishing (`ses_cech_h1`)

This section assembles the {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing into the surjectivity statement
`ses_cech_h1` (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`, Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).  The
{\v C}ech-algebra heart is `sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology` (the
cocycle-to-coboundary extraction proven above); the remaining content is the standard
sheaf theory — local lifting, left-exactness on sections, and gluing — which here is
supplied through Mathlib's `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` and a handful of
restriction-bookkeeping helpers.

Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules, so
the surjectivity criterion phrased through `sectionCechComplex` is new here. -/

section SesCechH1

open AlgebraicTopology TopologicalSpace Opposite

/-- **Composite of two presheaf restrictions is the direct restriction.**
For `A ≤ B ≤ C` the restriction `P(C) → P(B)` followed by `P(B) → P(A)` equals the direct
restriction `P(C) → P(A)`.  Holds definitionally for `Ab`-valued presheaves on opens
because the composite of the opposite `homOfLE`s is the opposite `homOfLE` of the
composite.  Used pervasively to collapse restriction chains in `ses_cech_h1`. -/
private lemma restr_trans (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {A B C : Opens ↥X}
    (h1 : A ≤ B) (h2 : B ≤ C) (x : ToType (P.obj (op C))) :
    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h1).op)
        (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h2).op) x)
      = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE (h1.trans h2)).op) x := by
  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← P.map_comp, ← op_comp]
  rfl

/-- **A morphism of presheaves of modules commutes with the {\v C}ech face restrictions.**
For `fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf` the section-level map `fι.app` intertwines the
{\v C}ech face restriction `sectionCechFaceRestr` of `F` with that of `G`; this is just
the naturality of `fι`.  It is the single naturality input that pushes the cocycle and
coboundary identities of the heart between `F` and `G`. -/
private lemma fι_sectionCechFaceRestr {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X)
    (F G : X.PresheafOfModules) (fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf)
    {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2))
    (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj
      (op (⨅ l, U ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l))))) :
    ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
        (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) x)
      = ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _)
          (ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i) x) := by
  unfold sectionCechFaceRestr
  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, fι.naturality]

/-- The single-index {\v C}ech intersection at a constant tuple is the cover member. -/
private lemma coverConst_iInf {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (i : ι) :
    (⨅ k : Fin 1, U ((fun _ => i) k)) = U i := by simp

/-- The double-index {\v C}ech intersection at `![i, j]` is the binary intersection. -/
private lemma coverPair_iInf {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (i j : ι) :
    (⨅ k : Fin 2, U (![i, j] k)) = U i ⊓ U j := by
  apply le_antisymm
  · exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (iInf_le _ 1)
  · exact le_iInf (fun k => by fin_cases k <;> simp)

/-- The `0`-th face of `![i, j]` is the constant tuple `j`. -/
private lemma pair_comp_δ0 {ι : Type u} (i j : ι) :
    (![i, j] ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ (0 : Fin 2)).toOrderHom) = (fun _ => j) := by
  funext k; fin_cases k; simp [SimplexCategory.δ, Fin.succAbove]

/-- The `1`-st face of `![i, j]` is the constant tuple `i`. -/
private lemma pair_comp_δ1 {ι : Type u} (i j : ι) :
    (![i, j] ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ (1 : Fin 2)).toOrderHom) = (fun _ => i) := by
  funext k; fin_cases k; simp [SimplexCategory.δ, Fin.succAbove]

end SesCechH1

end AlgebraicGeometry

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechBridge
open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Opposite Limits AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicTopology
universe u
variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

theorem ses_test {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X)
    (F G H : X.PresheafOfModules)
    (fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf) (gπ : G.presheaf ⟶ H.presheaf)
    (hGsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf)
    (hHsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf)
    (hπι : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj V)),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) x) = 0)
    (hmono : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V)))
    (hker : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (G.presheaf.obj V)),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) x = 0 →
        ∃ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) y = x)
    (s : ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))))
    (hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
    (sLoc : ∀ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))))
    (hlift : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (sLoc i)
      = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s) :
    ∃ g : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g = s := by
  classical
  -- single-index local sections, in ⨅-coordinates
  set sLoc'coord : (σ : Fin 1 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
    fun σ => ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map (homOfLE (iInf_le _ 0)).op) (sLoc (σ 0))
    with hsLoc'coord
  set sLoc' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U G).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) :=
    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).symm sLoc'coord with hsLoc'
  have hpe0 : sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0 sLoc' = sLoc'coord :=
    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).apply_symm_apply sLoc'coord
  -- degree-1 cochain (the G-differences)
  set cyc1 := ConcreteCategory.hom
    (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 0) sLoc' with hcyc1
  set dGcoord : (σ : Fin 2 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
    fun σ => sectionCechProductEquiv U G 1 cyc1 σ with hdGcoord
  have hdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, dGcoord σ
      = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
    intro σ
    rw [hdGcoord, hcyc1, sectionCech_objD_apply U G 0 sLoc' σ, hpe0]
  sorry

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechBridge
open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Opposite Limits AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicTopology
universe u
variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
theorem ses_test {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X)
    (F G H : X.PresheafOfModules)
    (fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf) (gπ : G.presheaf ⟶ H.presheaf)
    (hGsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf)
    (hHsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf)
    (hπι : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj V)),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) x) = 0)
    (hmono : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V)))
    (hker : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (G.presheaf.obj V)),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) x = 0 → ∃ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) y = x)
    (s : ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))))
    (hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
    (sLoc : ∀ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))))
    (hlift : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (sLoc i)
      = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s) :
    ∃ g : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g = s := by
  classical
  set sLoc'coord : (σ : Fin 1 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
    fun σ => ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map (homOfLE (iInf_le _ 0)).op) (sLoc (σ 0))
    with hsLoc'coord
  set sLoc' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U G).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) :=
    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).symm sLoc'coord with hsLoc'
  have hpe0 : sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0 sLoc' = sLoc'coord :=
    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).apply_symm_apply sLoc'coord
  set cyc1 := ConcreteCategory.hom
    (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 0) sLoc' with hcyc1
  set dGcoord : (σ : Fin 2 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
    fun σ => sectionCechProductEquiv U G 1 cyc1 σ with hdGcoord
  have hdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, dGcoord σ
      = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
    intro σ; rw [hdGcoord, hcyc1, sectionCech_objD_apply U G 0 sLoc' σ, hpe0]
  -- gπ of a single ⨅-coordinate local section = restriction of s from iSup
  have hgπsLoc' : ∀ (ρ : Fin 1 → ι) (h : (⨅ k, U (ρ k)) ≤ iSup U),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)))) (sLoc'coord ρ)
        = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op) s := by
    intro ρ h
    rw [hsLoc'coord, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, gπ.naturality,
      ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, hlift (ρ 0)]
    exact restr_trans H.presheaf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U (ρ 0)) s
  -- gπ kills the differences
  have hgπdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι,
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) (dGcoord σ) = 0 := by
    intro σ
    rw [hdG, map_sum]
    have key : ∀ i : Fin 2,
        ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
          ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)))
        = (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map
            (homOfLE (le_trans (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))
              (le_iSup U ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) 0))
                : (⨅ k, U (σ k)) ≤ iSup U)).op) s := by
      intro i
      rw [map_zsmul]; congr 1
      rw [fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U G H gπ σ i,
        hgπsLoc' _ (le_trans (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U _))]
      unfold sectionCechFaceRestr; rw [restr_trans]
    rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => key i), ← Finset.sum_smul,
      show (∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ)) = 0 by decide, zero_smul]
  -- lift each difference to F
  have hc : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, ∃ y : ToType (F.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) y = dGcoord σ :=
    fun σ => hker _ _ (hgπdG σ)
  choose c hcspec using hc
  -- the G-cocycle (d² = 0)
  have hGcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι, ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
        (dGcoord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0 := by
    intro σ
    have happ := sectionCech_objD_apply U G 1 cyc1 σ
    simp only [hdGcoord]
    rw [← happ]
    have hzero : ConcreteCategory.hom
        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 1) cyc1 = 0 := by
      rw [hcyc1, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared]
      simp
    rw [hzero, sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]; simp
  -- the F-cocycle for c
  have hcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι, ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
        (c (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0 := by
    intro σ
    apply hmono (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))
    rw [map_sum, map_zero]
    rw [show (0 : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))) = _ from (hGcoc σ).symm]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
    rw [map_zsmul, fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U F G fι σ i, hcspec]
  -- coboundary extraction (the heart)
  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology U F hH1 c hcoc
  sorry

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechBridge
open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Opposite Limits AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicTopology
universe u
variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
-- hg'coc in isolation: corrected family is a G 0-cocycle
example {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (F G : X.PresheafOfModules)
    (fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf)
    (sLoc'coord : (σ : Fin 1 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))))
    (dGcoord : (σ : Fin 2 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))))
    (t : (σ : Fin 1 → ι) → ToType (F.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))))
    (c : (σ : Fin 2 → ι) → ToType (F.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))))
    (hdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, dGcoord σ
      = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)))
    (hcspec : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) (c σ) = dGcoord σ)
    (ht : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, c σ = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
          (t (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)))
    (σ : Fin 2 → ι) :
    (∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
        ((fun ρ => sLoc'coord ρ - ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) (t ρ))
          (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom))) = 0 := by
  simp only [map_sub, smul_sub, Finset.sum_sub_distrib, ← hdG]
  rw [← hcspec σ, ht σ, map_sum]
  congr 1
  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
  rw [map_zsmul, fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U F G fι σ i]

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechBridge
open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Opposite Limits AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicTopology
universe u
variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
-- restriction between equal opens is injective
private lemma restr_inj_of_eq (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {V W : Opens ↥X}
    (h : V ≤ W) (h' : W ≤ V) :
    Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h).op)) := by
  intro a b hab
  have h2 := congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h').op)) hab
  rw [restr_trans, restr_trans] at h2
  rwa [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (h'.trans h)) (𝟙 _), op_id, P.map_id,
    ConcreteCategory.id_apply, ConcreteCategory.id_apply] at h2

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechBridge
open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Opposite Limits AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicTopology
universe u
variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
private lemma restr_inj_of_eq (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {V W : Opens ↥X}
    (h : V ≤ W) (h' : W ≤ V) :
    Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h).op)) := by
  intro a b hab
  have h2 := congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h').op)) hab
  rw [restr_trans, restr_trans] at h2
  rwa [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (h'.trans h)) (𝟙 _), op_id, P.map_id,
    ConcreteCategory.id_apply, ConcreteCategory.id_apply] at h2

example {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (G H : X.PresheafOfModules)
    (gπ : G.presheaf ⟶ H.presheaf)
    (hHsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf)
    (s : ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))))
    (gGlue : ∀ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))))
    (g : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))))
    (hgluing : TopCat.Presheaf.IsGluing (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf U gGlue g)
    -- gπ of each gGlue is the restriction of s
    (hgπglue : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (gGlue i)
      = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s) :
    ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g = s := by
  set sH : ∀ i, ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (U i))) :=
    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s with hsH
  have hcompatH : TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf U sH := by
    intro i j
    rw [hsH]
    simp only
    rw [restr_trans, restr_trans]
  have hg2 : TopCat.Presheaf.IsGluing (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf U sH s := fun i => rfl
  have hg1 : TopCat.Presheaf.IsGluing (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf U sH
      (ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g) := by
    intro i
    show ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op)
        (ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g) = sH i
    rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← gπ.naturality, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, hgluing i,
      hgπglue i]
  exact (hHsh.isSheafUniqueGluing U sH hcompatH).unique hg1 hg2

  rw [Fin.sum_univ_two] at h

/-- **Restriction between two equal opens is injective.**  When `V ≤ W` and `W ≤ V`
(i.e. `V = W`) the restriction `P(W) → P(V)` is injective: the reverse restriction is a
two-sided inverse (`restr_trans` collapses the round trip to the identity, by uniqueness of
poset morphisms).  Used to transport the {\v C}ech compatibility equation between the binary
intersection `U i ⊓ U j` and the {\v C}ech double-overlap `⨅ₖ U (![i,j] k)`. -/
private lemma restr_inj_of_eq (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {V W : Opens ↥X}
    (h : V ≤ W) (h' : W ≤ V) :
    Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h).op)) := by
  intro a b hab
  have h2 := congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h').op)) hab
  rw [restr_trans, restr_trans] at h2
  rwa [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (h'.trans h)) (𝟙 _), op_id, P.map_id,
    ConcreteCategory.id_apply, ConcreteCategory.id_apply] at h2
