/-- The `1`-st face of `![i, j]` is the constant tuple `i`. -/
private lemma pair_comp_δ1 {ι : Type u} (i j : ι) :
    (![i, j] ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ (1 : Fin 2)).toOrderHom) = (fun _ => i) := by
  funext k; fin_cases k; simp [SimplexCategory.δ, Fin.succAbove]

/-- **Surjectivity on sections from {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing** (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`,
Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).

For a short exact sequence of presheaves of modules `0 → F → G → H → 0` on a scheme `X`
— presented through the underlying `Ab`-presheaf maps `fι`, `gπ`, with `fι` a monomorphism
on sections (`hmono`), `gπ ∘ fι = 0` (`hπι`), and every kernel section of `gπ` in the image
of `fι` (`hker`) — together with an open cover `U` of the open `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ` such that
`Ȟ¹(U, F) = 0` (`hH1`) and a family of local lifts `sLoc i ∈ G(Uᵢ)` of `s ∈ H(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ)`
(`hlift`), the section `s` lies in the image of `G(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ) → H(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ)`.

The differences `sⱼ|_{ij} − sᵢ|_{ij}` of the local lifts map to `0` in `H`, hence lift to a
{\v C}ech `1`-cocycle in `F` (`hker`); the {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing makes it a coboundary
(`sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology`); subtracting the coboundary makes the
corrected lifts agree on overlaps, and the sheaf condition on `G` glues them to a global
section of `G` over `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ` mapping to `s` (separatedness of `H` identifies its image with
`s`).  The "cofinal system of covers" of the Stacks statement is captured here by taking a
single cover satisfying both `Ȟ¹ = 0` and the local-lift property as hypotheses.

Project-local: the {\v C}ech surjectivity criterion phrased through `sectionCechComplex` is
new here (Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules). -/
theorem ses_cech_h1 {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X)
    (F G H : X.PresheafOfModules)
    (fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf) (gπ : G.presheaf ⟶ H.presheaf)
    (hGsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf)
    (hHsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf)
    (hπι : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj V)),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) x) = 0)
    (hmono : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V)))
    (hker : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (G.presheaf.obj V)),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) x = 0 → ∃ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) y = x)
    (s : ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))))
    (hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
    (sLoc : ∀ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))))
    (hlift : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (sLoc i)
      = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s) :
    ∃ g : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g = s := by
  classical
  -- single-index local sections, recast in ⨅-coordinates
  set sLoc'coord : (σ : Fin 1 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
    fun σ => ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map (homOfLE (iInf_le _ 0)).op) (sLoc (σ 0))
    with hsLoc'coord
  set sLoc' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U G).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) :=
    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).symm sLoc'coord with hsLoc'
  have hpe0 : sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0 sLoc' = sLoc'coord :=
    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).apply_symm_apply sLoc'coord
  -- the degree-1 cochain of G-differences
  set cyc1 := ConcreteCategory.hom
    (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 0) sLoc' with hcyc1
  set dGcoord : (σ : Fin 2 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
    fun σ => sectionCechProductEquiv U G 1 cyc1 σ with hdGcoord
  have hdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, dGcoord σ
      = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
    intro σ; rw [hdGcoord, hcyc1, sectionCech_objD_apply U G 0 sLoc' σ, hpe0]
  -- `gπ` carries a single ⨅-coordinate local section to the restriction of `s`
  have hgπsLoc' : ∀ (ρ : Fin 1 → ι) (h : (⨅ k, U (ρ k)) ≤ iSup U),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)))) (sLoc'coord ρ)
        = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op) s := by
    intro ρ h
    rw [hsLoc'coord, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, gπ.naturality,
      ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, hlift (ρ 0)]
    exact restr_trans H.presheaf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U (ρ 0)) s
  -- `gπ` kills the differences
  have hgπdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι,
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) (dGcoord σ) = 0 := by
    intro σ
    rw [hdG, map_sum]
    have key : ∀ i : Fin 2,
        ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
          ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)))
        = (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map
            (homOfLE (le_trans (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))
              (le_iSup U ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) 0))
                : (⨅ k, U (σ k)) ≤ iSup U)).op) s := by
      intro i
      rw [map_zsmul]; congr 1
      rw [fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U G H gπ σ i,
        hgπsLoc' _ (le_trans (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U _))]
      unfold sectionCechFaceRestr; rw [restr_trans]
    rw [Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => key i), ← Finset.sum_smul,
      show (∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ)) = 0 by decide, zero_smul]
  -- lift each difference into `F`
  have hc : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, ∃ y : ToType (F.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) y = dGcoord σ :=
    fun σ => hker _ _ (hgπdG σ)
  choose c hcspec using hc
  -- the `G`-side cocycle identity (`d² = 0`)
  have hGcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι, ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
        (dGcoord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0 := by
    intro σ
    have happ := sectionCech_objD_apply U G 1 cyc1 σ
    simp only [hdGcoord]
    rw [← happ]
    have hzero : ConcreteCategory.hom
        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 1) cyc1 = 0 := by
      rw [hcyc1, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared]
      simp
    rw [hzero, sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]; simp
  -- the `F`-side cocycle identity for the lifted differences
  have hcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι, ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
        (c (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0 := by
    intro σ
    apply hmono (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))
    rw [map_sum, map_zero]
    rw [show (0 : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))) = _ from (hGcoc σ).symm]
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
    rw [map_zsmul, fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U F G fι σ i, hcspec]
  -- coboundary extraction (the {\v C}ech-`H¹` heart)
  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology U F hH1 c hcoc
  -- the corrected family in ⨅-coordinates
  set g'coord : (ρ : Fin 1 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)))) :=
    fun ρ => sLoc'coord ρ - ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) (t ρ) with hg'coord
  have hg'coc : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
        (g'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0 := by
    intro σ
    simp only [hg'coord, map_sub, smul_sub, Finset.sum_sub_distrib, ← hdG]
    rw [← hcspec σ, ht σ, map_sum]
    congr 1
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
    rw [map_zsmul, fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U F G fι σ i]
  -- pairwise agreement of the corrected family on double overlaps
  have hagree2 : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι,
      ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ 0)
        (g'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ 0).toOrderHom))
      = ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ 1)
        (g'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ 1).toOrderHom)) := by
    intro σ
    have h := hg'coc σ
    rw [Fin.sum_univ_two] at h
    simp only [pow_zero, one_smul, pow_one, neg_one_zsmul] at h
    rwa [add_neg_eq_zero] at h
  -- the `U`-indexed corrected family
  set gGlue : ∀ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))) :=
    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom
      (G.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_of_eq (coverConst_iInf U i).symm)).op) (g'coord (fun _ => i))
    with hgGlue
  have hcompat : TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf U gGlue := by
    intro i j
    simp only [hgGlue]
    apply restr_inj_of_eq G.presheaf (le_of_eq (coverPair_iInf U i j).symm)
      (le_of_eq (coverPair_iInf U i j))
    simp only
    rw [restr_trans, restr_trans, restr_trans, restr_trans]
    have key := hagree2 ![i, j]
    unfold sectionCechFaceRestr at key
    rw [pair_comp_δ0, pair_comp_δ1] at key
    rw [restr_trans, restr_trans] at key
    rw [← key]
  -- glue the corrected family to a global section of `G`
  obtain ⟨g, hgluing, -⟩ := hGsh.isSheafUniqueGluing U gGlue hcompat
  refine ⟨g, ?_⟩
  -- `gπ g = s` by separatedness of `H`
  set sH : ∀ i, ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (U i))) :=
    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s with hsH
  have hcompatH : TopCat.Presheaf.IsCompatible (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf U sH := by
    intro i j; simp only [hsH]; rw [restr_trans, restr_trans]
  have hg2 : TopCat.Presheaf.IsGluing (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf U sH s := fun i => rfl
  have hgπglue : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (gGlue i) = sH i := by
    intro i
    simp only [hgGlue, hsH, hg'coord]
    rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, gπ.naturality, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, map_sub,
      hπι, sub_zero, hgπsLoc' _ ((coverConst_iInf U i).le.trans (le_iSup U i)), restr_trans]
  have hg1 : TopCat.Presheaf.IsGluing (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf U sH
      (ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g) := by
    intro i
    show ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op)
        (ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g) = sH i
    rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← gπ.naturality, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, hgluing i,
      hgπglue i]
  exact (hHsh.isSheafUniqueGluing U sH hcompatH).unique hg1 hg2

end SesCechH1

name-agnostic variant of `restr_trans`: works for *any* opposite-opens morphisms (not only
syntactic `homOfLE`), using uniqueness of poset morphisms.  This is the robust form used to
collapse restriction chains involving `Opens.infLELeft`/`Opens.leSupr`/`sectionCechFaceRestr`,
none of which are syntactically `homOfLE`. -/

    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map f) (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map g) x)
      = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map h) x := by
  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← P.map_comp,
    show g ≫ f = h from Quiver.Hom.unop_inj (Subsingleton.elim _ _)]

/-- **Two parallel restrictions agree.**  Any two restriction maps `P(A) → P(C)` (opposite
opens morphisms) act identically, by uniqueness of poset morphisms.  Used to discharge the
final defeq-up-to-proof-irrelevance step after collapsing restriction chains with
`← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp`. -/
private lemma restr_op_unique (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {A C : Opens ↥X}
    (f g : Opposite.op C ⟶ Opposite.op A) (x : ToType (P.obj (Opposite.op C))) :
    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map f) x = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map g) x := by
  rw [show f = g from Quiver.Hom.unop_inj (Subsingleton.elim _ _)]

632	Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).
633	
634	For a short exact sequence of presheaves of modules `0 → F → G → H → 0` on a scheme `X`
635	— presented through the underlying `Ab`-presheaf maps `fι`, `gπ`, with `fι` a monomorphism
636	on sections (`hmono`), `gπ ∘ fι = 0` (`hπι`), and every kernel section of `gπ` in the image
637	of `fι` (`hker`) — together with an open cover `U` of the open `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ` such that
638	`Ȟ¹(U, F) = 0` (`hH1`) and a family of local lifts `sLoc i ∈ G(Uᵢ)` of `s ∈ H(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ)`
639	(`hlift`), the section `s` lies in the image of `G(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ) → H(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ)`.
640	
641	The differences `sⱼ|_{ij} − sᵢ|_{ij}` of the local lifts map to `0` in `H`, hence lift to a
642	{\v C}ech `1`-cocycle in `F` (`hker`); the {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing makes it a coboundary
643	(`sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology`); subtracting the coboundary makes the
644	corrected lifts agree on overlaps, and the sheaf condition on `G` glues them to a global
645	section of `G` over `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ` mapping to `s` (separatedness of `H` identifies its image with
646	`s`).  The "cofinal system of covers" of the Stacks statement is captured here by taking a
647	single cover satisfying both `Ȟ¹ = 0` and the local-lift property as hypotheses.
648	
649	Project-local: the {\v C}ech surjectivity criterion phrased through `sectionCechComplex` is
650	new here (Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules). -/

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechBridge.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (11): the named Lane-2 target `ses_cech_h1` is BUILT and AXIOM-CLEAN.**
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.ses_cech_h1` (theorem, ~line 668) — `#print axioms` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.
  - 10 supporting `private` lemmas (all axiom-clean): `restr_trans`, `restr_inj_of_eq`,
    `restr_op_unique`, `restr_g'_transport`, `fι_sectionCechFaceRestr`, `coverConst_iInf`,
    `coverPair_iInf`, `pair_comp_δ0`, `pair_comp_δ1`.
- **Blocked: none.** Lane 2 closed.
- **sorry count: 0 → 0** across the file. Full-file `lake env lean` = EXIT 0, no errors/warnings.

## `ses_cech_h1` (line 668) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement (faithful to blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1` / Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`):** for a SES
  of presheaves of modules `0→F→G→H→0` on a scheme `X` — presented as underlying `Ab`-presheaf
  maps `fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf`, `gπ : G.presheaf ⟶ H.presheaf` with
  - `hmono` (`fι` injective on sections),
  - `hπι` (`gπ∘fι = 0` on sections),
  - `hker` (kernel sections of `gπ` lift through `fι`),
  - `hGsh`, `hHsh` (`G.presheaf`, `H.presheaf` are sheaves, via
    `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat)`) —
  together with a cover `U : ι → Opens ↥X`, `hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1)`,
  and local lifts `sLoc i ∈ G(Uᵢ)` of `s ∈ H(iSup U)` (`hlift`), the conclusion is
  `∃ g : G(iSup U), gπ(g) = s` (surjectivity on sections).
- **Design note for the planner:** the blueprint's "cofinal system of covers" is captured by taking
  a SINGLE cover that simultaneously satisfies `Ȟ¹(U,F)=0` (`hH1`) and the local-lift property
  (`sLoc`/`hlift`) as hypotheses. Given the cover, the argument IS the full Stacks proof. The
  "cofinality ⟹ can choose such a cover" refinement is a separate (downstream) step. This is the
  honest content of the lemma; the signature is faithful and consumable by `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`.

     restrict the single `s` ⟹ alternating sum `∑(-1)^i = 0`.

     pairwise-agrees on double overlaps (`hagree2`).
  8. Recast to a `U`-indexed glue family `gGlue`, prove `IsCompatible` (`hcompat`), glue via
     `hGsh.isSheafUniqueGluing` to `g`, then `gπ(g)=s` by `H`-separatedness

Stop when you have tried alternatives and cannot make further axiom-clean progress. Before stopping:

- Commit all axiom-clean steps you completed.
- Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with a precise handoff (see Logging below).
- Ensure the file compiles with no `sorry`.

## File structure

Place new declarations under a clearly delimited section:

```lean
/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — <TopicName> -/
```

- Use `private` for helpers with no downstream use outside this file.
- Use `theorem` / `lemma` (non-private) for steps that other files will import.
- Add a one-line docstring to each non-private declaration explaining why it is project-local.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any existing declaration. You may add new declarations freely. You must not rename, re-type, or modify signatures of protected declarations.

## API alignment

Mathlib names and signatures are the most common failure source.

- Verify every name with `lean_local_search` or `lean_loogle` — do not rely on memory.
- Check that typeclass assumptions in your statement match what Mathlib requires.
- For `def`-backed types: Lean may not unfold them for typeclass synthesis; use explicit coercions or `change` / `show` in the proof body.

## Write permissions

Only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror the `.lean` path):

```markdown
# Algebra/MathlibSupplement.lean

## Session summary
- Built axiom-clean: `Foo.bar`, `Foo.baz` (lines 12–40)
- Blocked on: `Foo.qux` — needs `Bar.aux` which requires [precise statement]

## Foo.bar (line 12)
- **Approach:** Direct from `Mathlib.Algebra.X` via `simp [Y]`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean

## Foo.qux (not added)
- **Approach 1:** Tried [route] — FAILED because [reason]
- **Approach 2:** Tried informal agent — suggested [sketch], formalization blocked on [specific gap]
- **Next step:** Build `Bar.aux : [precise type statement]` first
- **Dead end:** Do not retry [route] — [why it fails]
```

One section per declaration attempted. For each: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDED), dead-end warnings, next step.

## End-of-session handoff

**Write a `## Summary` section** before the verdicts: declarations added (count + names), declarations blocked (count + why), sorry count before → after across your file.

**Write a `## Needs blueprint entry` section.** This mode is the biggest source of blueprint debt: every non-private definition/lemma you add is, by construction, infrastructure with **no blueprint block yet** — a `lean_aux` node invisible to the dependency graph (`archon dag-query unmatched` will list it) until the planner/reviewer blueprints it. - `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt them — name each. This is valid but weak; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
159

8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech

10	/-!
11	# Free-presheaf Čech complex (P3b bridge — free-complex side)
12	
13	This file is the free-presheaf-complex side of the P3b bridge.  The section side lives in
14	`PresheafCech.lean`; this file owns the two free-complex declarations:
15	
16	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex`  (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`)
17	  — the chain complex of free presheaves of `O_X`-modules whose degree-`p` term is
18	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj`
19	  `  (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`.
20	
21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`  (`lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`)
22	  — the free complex is a quasi-isomorphism / free resolution of `O_𝒰`.
23	  **(not yet built — the degreewise chain iso `cechFreeEvalEngineIso` to the combinatorial engine
24	  model is now complete and axiom-clean, together with the engine acyclicity and augmentation; the
25	  remaining step is the nonempty-case `QuasiIso` of `cechEngineComplexAug` and its transfer.)**
26	
27	The quasi-isomorphism is proved objectwise (`quasiIso_of_evaluation`): for each open `V`,
28	the evaluated augmented complex is the augmented combinatorial Čech complex of the full
29	simplex on `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` with constant coefficients `O_X(V)`, which is
30	contractible when `I₁(V) ≠ ∅` (via the prepend-`i_fix` homotopy
31	`FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy`) and zero when `I₁(V) = ∅`.  This file owns the
32	combinatorial engine (`FreeCechEngine`) supplying that contracting homotopy and the
33	per-`V` sectionwise reductions building toward `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`.
34	-/
35	
36	universe u
37	
38	open CategoryTheory Limits
39	
40	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
41	
42	/-
43	Planner strategy (P3b free-complex side; see analogies/p3b-presheafcech.md +
44	blueprint §Presheaf-level Čech machinery):
45	
46	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
47	`cechFreePresheafComplex`
48	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
49	Goal: a `ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` whose degree-`p` term is
50	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`
51	with the alternating-face differential.
52	
53	Recommended build path — use the simplicial route so that d²=0 comes for free:
54	1. Construct a `SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules` whose `n`-simplices are the
55	   above direct sums.
56	2. Apply `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` to obtain the chain complex.
57	   This gives d²=0 automatically via `SimplicialObject.boundarySquareZero`.
58	
59	Key API:
60	- `PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj :`
61	  `  ((Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
62	  — the free-presheaf-of-modules functor.  Use it as `(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj` and
63	  `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map`.
64	- `yoneda.obj V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` — the representable presheaf of sets at `V`.
65	  Do NOT introduce a bespoke `j_!`; `free ∘ yoneda` is the correct substitute.
66	- `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` — turns a simplicial abelian group (or
67	  simplicial object in an abelian category) into a chain complex; d²=0 is a theorem.
68	- Direct sums: `⨁` is `DirectSum`; in `PresheafOfModules` use
69	  `Limits.biproduct` / `DirectSum.lof` / `DirectSum.desc`.
70	
71	DEAD END — do NOT hand-roll the alternating-sum identity for d²=0.  Use the simplicial
72	path above.
73	
74	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
76	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
77	Goal: show that `cechFreePresheafComplex` → `O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism (i.e.,
78	the free complex is a free resolution of the structure sheaf restricted to the cover).
79	
80	Recommended build path — objectwise contracting homotopy:
81	1. Homology in `X.PresheafOfModules` is computed objectwise (colimits are objectwise).
82	   So it suffices to exhibit, for each open `V : Opens ↥X`, a contracting homotopy on
83	   the sectionwise complex `cechFreePresheafComplex(V)`.
84	2. Sectionwise at `V`, degree `p` is `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} R(V)` for those multi-indices
85	   `σ` with `V ⊆ ⨅ k, U (σ k)`, and `0` otherwise.
86	3. Fix any `i_fix : ι` with `V ⊆ U i_fix` (if none exists, the complex is `0`).
87	   The homotopy `h : K_p(V) → K_{p+1}(V)` maps the `σ`-summand to the `(i_fix, σ)`-summand
88	   (prepend `i_fix`).  Check `dh + hd = id` at each degree.
89	4. Package as `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy`, then use `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso`.
90	
91	Key API:
92	- `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` — `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Homotopy`.
93	- `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` (or `Homotopy.toQuasiIso`) — homotopy equivalence ⟹ quasi-iso.
94	
95	DEAD END — do NOT route through `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy`.  That
96	interface has a different index convention and is not directly applicable here.
97	-/
98	
99	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — free-presheaf Čech complex
100	
101	This section builds the free-presheaf Čech complex `cechFreePresheafComplex` of a finite
102	open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover` as the alternating-face-map chain complex of an explicit
103	simplicial object `cechFreeSimplicial`.  Everything is project-local: Mathlib has the
104	free-presheaf-of-modules functor, the Yoneda embedding and `alternatingFaceMapComplex`,
105	but not their assembly into the Čech free resolution of a cover.
106	
107	We require `[Finite 𝒰.I₀]` so that the index types `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` of the degreewise
108	coproducts are finite (`HasCoproductsOfShape`); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of
109	the downstream protected theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`. -/
110	
111	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
112	
113	/-- **Free presheaf of modules on a representable open.**
114	
115	The composite `Opens X --yoneda--> ((Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type) --free--> X.PresheafOfModules`.
116	For an open `V`, `freeYoneda.obj V` is the extension-by-zero free presheaf of modules
117	`(j_V)_! O_X|_V` of the blueprint: on sections over `W` it is the free `O_X(W)`-module on
118	`Hom(W, V)`, i.e. `O_X(W)` if `W ⊆ V` and `0` otherwise.
119	
120	Project-local because Mathlib provides `PresheafOfModules.free` and `yoneda` separately but
121	not this composite, which is the basic building block of the free Čech complex. -/
122	noncomputable def freeYoneda : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules :=
123	  yoneda ⋙ PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj
124	
125	/-- The open underlying the `i`-th member of an open cover, as `(𝒰.f i).opensRange`. -/
126	def coverOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (i : 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X := (𝒰.f i).opensRange
127	
128	/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`.
129	
130	For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
131	degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
132	def coverInterOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
133	  ⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)
134	
135	/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
136	`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
137	representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
138	lemma coverInterOpen_comp_le (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
139	    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α) :=
140	  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)
141	
142	/-- A coproduct injection transported along an equality of indices: `eqToHom` of the induced
143	object equality cancels the index change. A generic categorical helper used to discharge the
144	dependent-index bookkeeping in the simplicial identities of `cechFreeSimplicial`. -/
145	private lemma sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport {C : Type*} [Category C] {β : Type*} (B : β → C)
146	    [HasCoproduct B] {a b : β} (e : a = b) :
147	    eqToHom (congrArg B e) ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι B b = Limits.Sigma.ι B a := by
148	  subst e; simp
149	
150	/-- **The free Čech simplicial object of a finite open cover.**
151	
152	The simplicial object in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is the coproduct
153	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and whose simplicial maps
154	reindex the multi-index `σ ↦ σ ∘ α` (along the order map of `α : [p] ⟶ [q]`) together with
155	the representable index-dropping inclusion `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α)`.
156	
157	Taking its `alternatingFaceMapComplex` produces `cechFreePresheafComplex` with `d² = 0` for
158	free (the simplicial route avoids hand-rolling the alternating-sum identity).
159	
160	Project-local: this is the simplicial backbone of the free Čech resolution; it has no
161	Mathlib counterpart. -/
162	noncomputable def cechFreeSimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
163	    SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules where
164	  obj n := ∐ fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
165	  map {n m} α := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
166	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 α.unop.toOrderHom σ)) ≫
167	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (m.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))
168	        (σ ∘ α.unop.toOrderHom)
169	  map_id n := by
170	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
171	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Category.comp_id]
172	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) = σ := by funext i; simp
173	    have eo : coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop))
174	        = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ := by rw [e]
175	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE
176	        (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 (SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) σ)) (eqToHom eo.symm),
177	      eqToHom_map]
178	    exact sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
179	      (fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) e
180	  map_comp {n m k} f g := by
181	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
182	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
183	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (f ≫ g).unop)
184	        = (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f.unop))
185	            ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom g.unop) := by
186	      funext i; simp [unop_comp, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom]
187	    rw [← sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
188	          (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e,
189	      show (eqToHom (congrArg (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
190	              freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e))
191	          = freeYoneda.map (eqToHom (congrArg (coverInterOpen 𝒰) e)) from (eqToHom_map _ _).symm]
192	    simp only [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
193	    congr 2
194	
195	/-- **The free-presheaf Čech complex of a finite open cover** (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`).
196	
197	The chain complex `K(𝒰)_•` in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is
198	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
199	`= ⨁_σ (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`, with the
200	alternating-sum index-dropping differential. Built as the `alternatingFaceMapComplex` of
201	`cechFreeSimplicial`, so `d² = 0` holds by the simplicial identities.
202	
203	Project-local: the free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
204	noncomputable def cechFreePresheafComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
205	    ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ :=
206	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex X.PresheafOfModules).obj (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰)
207	
208	/-- Degreewise unfolding of the free Čech complex: the degree-`p` term is the coproduct of
209	free presheaves over the `(p+1)`-fold multi-indices. Exposed (it holds by `rfl`) so that the
210	downstream hom-identification and quasi-isomorphism arguments can rewrite the degree-`p`
211	object without unfolding `alternatingFaceMapComplex` and `cechFreeSimplicial` by hand. -/
212	lemma cechFreePresheafComplex_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
213	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p
214	      = ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) :=
215	  rfl
216	
217	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — augmentation and cover structure presheaf -/
218	
219	/-- **Augmentation of a representable free presheaf onto the structure presheaf.**
220	
221	The canonical map `freeYoneda V ⟶ unit` (= `O_X`) corresponding under the free–Yoneda hom
222	bijection to the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)`. Concretely it sends the free generator over a
223	`W ⊆ V` to `1 ∈ O_X(W)`. This is the per-summand component of the Čech augmentation
224	`K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰`.
225	
226	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `freeYoneda` nor its augmentation. -/
227	noncomputable def freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
228	    freeYoneda.obj V ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
229	  (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).symm
230	    (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)))
231	
232	/-- Value of the free–Yoneda hom bijection on the augmentation: it is the unit section `1`. -/
233	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
234	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj) (freeYonedaAug V)
235	      = (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))) := by
236	  simp only [freeYonedaAug, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
237	
238	/-- Value of the augmentation `freeYoneda V' ⟶ O_X` on a generator `freeMk g` (`g : V ⟶ V'`):
239	it is the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)` (the restriction of `1 ∈ O_X(V')`). -/
240	private lemma freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (g : V ⟶ V') :
241	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk g)
242	      = (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
243	  have key := (freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply V' (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
244	      (freeYonedaAug V')).symm.trans (freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug V')
245	  have hmap : (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYoneda.obj V').map g.op))
246	      (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V')) = ModuleCat.freeMk g := by
247	    erw [PresheafOfModules.freeObj_map, ModuleCat.freeDesc_apply]
248	    change ModuleCat.freeMk ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((yoneda.obj V').map g.op)) (𝟙 V'))
249	      = ModuleCat.freeMk g
250	    congr 1
251	  have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply (freeYonedaAug V') g.op
252	    (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))
253	  rw [hmap] at hnat
254	  rw [hnat]
255	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op))
256	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V'))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))) = 1
257	  exact (congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op)) key).trans (map_one _)
258	
259	/-- **Naturality of the augmentation.** For `V ≤ V'` the restriction map of free presheaves
260	composed with the augmentation onto `O_X` equals the augmentation over the smaller open:
261	`freeYoneda.map (incl) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V`. This is the cocone condition
262	that turns the per-summand augmentations into a chain map `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
263	lemma freeYoneda_map_comp_aug {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ V') :
264	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE h) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V := by
265	  apply (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).injective
266	  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app]
267	  erw [ModuleCat.comp_apply, Functor.comp_map,
268	    PresheafOfModules.free_map_app, ModuleCat.free_map_apply, freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk]
269	
270	/-- **Degree-`0` augmentation of the free Čech complex.** The map
271	`K(𝒰)_0 = ∐_{σ : Fin 1 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X` assembled from the per-summand
272	augmentations `freeYonedaAug`. Its image presheaf is the cover structure presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
273	noncomputable def cechFreeAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
274	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X 0 ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
275	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
276	
277	/-- **The cover structure presheaf** `O_𝒰` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
278	
279	The image (as a presheaf of `O_X`-modules) of the degree-`0` augmentation `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_X`
280	(`cechFreeAug`). Concretely `O_𝒰(W) = O_X(W)` when `W` is contained in some cover member
281	`U_i`, and `0` otherwise. The free Čech complex `K(𝒰)_•` is a resolution of `O_𝒰`
282	(`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`).
283	
284	Project-local: the image presheaf of a cover's augmentation has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
285	noncomputable def coverStructurePresheaf (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
286	    X.PresheafOfModules :=
287	  Limits.image (cechFreeAug 𝒰)
288	
289	/-- Each face map `δ i : K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation is the same
290	multi-indexed augmentation `∐_{σ : Fin 2 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X`, independent of `i`.
291	This is the cosimplicial-identity input to `d ≫ aug = 0`: the two faces become equal after
292	augmenting, by naturality of `freeYonedaAug`. -/
293	private lemma cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (i : Fin 2) :
294	    (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰
295	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun σ : Fin 2 → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
296	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
297	  intro σ
298	  simp only [SimplicialObject.δ, cechFreeSimplicial, cechFreeAug, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
299	    Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.assoc]
300	  exact freeYoneda_map_comp_aug (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 _ σ)
301	
302	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation vanishes: the
303	alternating face sum `δ_0 - δ_1` is killed because both faces agree after augmenting
304	(`cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`). This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
305	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
306	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
307	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
308	  have hd : (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0
309	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0 :=
310	    AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex_obj_d (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
311	  -- Prove the simplicial-level identity as a standalone `have` so its composition `≫` is
312	  -- elaborated fresh at the `cechFreeSimplicial.obj` types — matching
313	  -- `cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`. Rewriting `hd` into the goal first would pin the composition
314	  -- at the `.X` types of the chain complex, which are only definitionally (not syntactically)
315	  -- equal and block the rewrites.
316	  have main : AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
317	      ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
318	    rw [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD, Fin.sum_univ_two,
319	      Preadditive.add_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp,
320	      cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug, cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug]
321	    simp only [Fin.isValue, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
322	    abel
323	  rw [hd]; exact main
324	
325	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the map onto the image presheaf
326	`O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` vanishes. Obtained from `cechFree_d_comp_aug` by cancelling the
327	mono `image.ι`. This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
328	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`cechFreeComplexAug`). -/
329	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
330	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) = 0 := by
331	  rw [← cancel_mono (Limits.image.ι (cechFreeAug 𝒰)), Category.assoc, Limits.image.fac,
332	    Limits.zero_comp]
333	  exact cechFree_d_comp_aug 𝒰
334	
335	/-- **The augmentation chain map** `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
336	
337	The chain map from the free Čech complex to the cover structure presheaf concentrated in degree
338	`0`, whose degree-`0` component is the canonical map `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` onto the
339	image presheaf. The chain-map condition `d ≫ aug = 0` is `cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage`. The
340	quasi-isomorphism claim `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` asserts this map is a quasi-isomorphism.
341	
342	Project-local: the augmented free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
343	noncomputable def cechFreeComplexAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
344	    cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰 ⟶
345	      (ChainComplex.single₀ X.PresheafOfModules).obj (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰) :=
346	  ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).toSingle₀Equiv (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰)).symm
347	    ⟨Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰), cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage 𝒰⟩
348	
349	/-- The degree-`0` component of the augmentation chain map is the canonical map onto the image
350	presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
351	lemma cechFreeComplexAug_f_zero (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
352	    (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).f 0 = Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) := by
353	  rw [cechFreeComplexAug, ChainComplex.toSingle₀Equiv_symm_apply_f_zero]
354	
355	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — objectwise detection of quasi-isomorphisms
356	
357	Homology in `PresheafOfModules R` is computed objectwise: the evaluation functors
358	`PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V` are jointly conservative and preserve homology, so a
359	morphism of complexes of presheaves of modules is a quasi-isomorphism as soon as each of its
360	evaluations is.  These three lemmas package that reduction.  Mathlib has the single-functor
361	statement `HomologicalComplex.quasiIso_map_iff_of_preservesHomology` (which needs the functor to
362	reflect isomorphisms — false for a single evaluation), but not the joint-conservativity version
363	needed to reduce a quasi-isomorphism of presheaf-of-module complexes to its sectionwise checks. -/
364	
365	open HomologicalComplex in
366	/-- For a functor `F` preserving homology, if the induced map on the homology of the mapped
367	complexes is an isomorphism, then `F` applied to the homology map is an isomorphism.  This is the
368	naturality square of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso` read off at a fixed degree. -/
369	private lemma isIso_Fmap_homologyMap {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} {C₁ C₂ : Type*}
370	    [Category C₁] [Category C₂] [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive C₂]
371	    {K L : HomologicalComplex C₁ c} (φ : K ⟶ L) (F : C₁ ⥤ C₂) [F.Additive] [F.PreservesHomology]
372	    (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i]
373	    [((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).HasHomology i]
374	    [((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj L).HasHomology i]
375	    (hiso : IsIso (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap ((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) i)) :
376	    IsIso (F.map (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap φ i)) := by
377	  have key := ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso_hom_naturality ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ) F
378	  haveI hmid : IsIso (ShortComplex.homologyMap
379	      (F.mapShortComplex.map ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ))) := hiso
380	  change IsIso (F.map (ShortComplex.homologyMap ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ)))
381	  haveI hcomp : IsIso (ShortComplex.homologyMap
382	      (F.mapShortComplex.map ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ)) ≫
383	      (((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj L).mapHomologyIso F).hom) := inferInstance
384	  haveI hcomp2 : IsIso ((((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj K).mapHomologyIso F).hom ≫
385	      F.map (ShortComplex.homologyMap ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ))) := key ▸ hcomp
386	  exact IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left
387	    (((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj K).mapHomologyIso F).hom _
388	
389	/-- **Joint conservativity of evaluations.** A morphism of presheaves of `R`-modules is an
390	isomorphism as soon as each of its evaluations `(evaluation R V).map g` is, since the underlying
391	presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of presheaves is an
392	isomorphism iff it is so objectwise.  Project-local: the joint-conservativity packaging is not in
393	Mathlib. -/
394	private lemma isIso_of_evaluation {C : Type*} [Category C] (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat)
395	    {M N : PresheafOfModules R} (g : M ⟶ N)
396	    (h : ∀ V, IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).map g)) : IsIso g := by
397	  suffices hh : IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).map g) from
398	    isIso_of_reflects_iso g (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R)
399	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
400	  intro V
401	  have hV := h V
402	  rw [PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map] at hV
403	  haveI : IsIso (g.app V) := hV
404	  have e : ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).map g).app V
405	      = (forget₂ (ModuleCat (R.obj V)) Ab).map (g.app V) := rfl
406	  rw [e]
407	  exact Functor.map_isIso (forget₂ (ModuleCat (R.obj V)) Ab) (g.app V)
408	
409	/-- **Objectwise reduction of quasi-isomorphisms.** A morphism `φ` of chain complexes of
410	presheaves of `R`-modules is a quasi-isomorphism if, for every object `V`, its evaluation
411	`(evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex |>.map φ` (a morphism of complexes of `R(V)`-modules) is a
412	quasi-isomorphism.  This is the formal core of the sectionwise argument that the free Čech complex
413	resolves the cover structure presheaf: homology of presheaves of modules is computed objectwise.
414	
415	Project-local: combines `isIso_of_evaluation` (joint conservativity) with `isIso_Fmap_homologyMap`
416	(evaluation preserves homology). -/
417	lemma quasiIso_of_evaluation {C : Type*} [Category C] (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat) {ι : Type*}
418	    {c : ComplexShape ι} {K L : HomologicalComplex (PresheafOfModules R) c} (φ : K ⟶ L)
419	    [∀ i, K.HasHomology i] [∀ i, L.HasHomology i]
420	    (h : ∀ V, QuasiIso (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ)) :
421	    QuasiIso φ := by
422	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
423	  intro i
424	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
425	  apply isIso_of_evaluation R
426	  intro V
427	  rw [PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map]
428	  haveI hqV : QuasiIsoAt (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) i :=
429	    (h V).quasiIsoAt i
430	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap] at hqV
431	  exact isIso_Fmap_homologyMap φ (PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V) i hqV
432	
433	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — combinatorial contracting-homotopy engine
434	
435	The objectwise reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation` turns `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` into a
436	per-open-`V` statement: the evaluation of the augmented free Čech complex at `V` is the
437	augmented combinatorial Čech complex of the full simplex on `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` with
438	constant coefficients the ring `O_X(V)`.  This section ports the constant-coefficient
439	combinatorial contracting homotopy — the prepend-`i_fix` map and its `d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id`
440	identity — into this file as a self-contained algebraic engine.
441	
442	This is the free-side analogue of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy` in `CechAcyclic.lean`
443	(those declarations are `private` there, hence unavailable here); the proofs are the same
444	alternating-sum cancellation, specialised to the constant coefficient module.  Here `M` is
445	the constant coefficient module (to be `O_X(V)` in the application) and `ι` the index type
446	(to be `I₁(V)`). -/
447	
448	namespace FreeCechEngine
449	
450	variable {ι : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ℕ}
451	
452	/-- Alternating coface (Čech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
453	`(d t)(σ) = ∑ⱼ (-1)ʲ • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)`, the alternating sum of the index-dropping
454	maps.  Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential`. -/
455	def combDifferential (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M :=
456	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
457	
458	/-- The contracting homotopy: prepend the fixed index `r`.  `(h u)(τ) = u (Fin.cons r τ)`.
459	Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy`. -/
460	def combHomotopy (r : ι) (u : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) : (Fin n → ι) → M :=
461	  fun τ => u (Fin.cons r τ)
462	
463	@[simp] lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : ι) :
464	    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
465	  funext τ; simp [combHomotopy]
466	
467	/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(k+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the `k`-th
468	coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
469	lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : ι) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
470	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
471	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := by
472	  funext l
473	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
474	  · simp
475	  · intro i; simp [Fin.succ_succAbove_succ]
476	
477	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** `d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id` on the constant-coefficient
478	Čech complex (Stacks `lemma-homology-complex`, the `dh + hd = id` computation).  Free-side
479	port of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy_spec`. -/
480	lemma combHomotopy_spec (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) :
481	    combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) + combHomotopy r (combDifferential t) = t := by
482	  funext σ
483	  simp only [combDifferential, combHomotopy, Pi.add_apply]
484	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun j : Fin (n + 2) =>
485	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ j.succAbove))]
486	  have h0 : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 2)).succAbove = σ := by
487	    funext i; simp
488	  rw [h0]
489	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
490	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
491	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
492	    rw [cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]; abel), add_zero]
493	
494	/-- Every cocycle is a coboundary in positive degree: if `d t = 0` then `t = d (h t)`. -/
495	lemma combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M)
496	    (ht : combDifferential t = 0) : combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) = t := by
497	  have h := combHomotopy_spec r t
498	  rw [ht, combHomotopy_zero, add_zero] at h
499	  exact h
500	
501	/-- Sign-cancellation behind `d² = 0`: under the index swap
502	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` the alternating sign flips. -/
503	lemma combSign_flip (j : Fin (n + 2)) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
504	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ)) * ((-1) ^ (i : ℕ))
505	      = - (((-1 : ℤ) ^ ((j.succAbove i : Fin (n + 2)) : ℕ))
506	            * ((-1) ^ ((i.predAbove j : Fin (n + 1)) : ℕ))) := by
507	  rcases lt_or_ge (i.castSucc) j with h | h
508	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h,
509	        Fin.val_castSucc, Fin.val_pred]
510	    have hpos : 0 < (j : ℕ) := lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.zero_le _) (by exact_mod_cast h)
511	    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ : ∃ m, (j : ℕ) = m + 1 := ⟨(j : ℕ) - 1, by omega⟩
512	    rw [hm]; simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel, pow_succ]; ring
513	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h,
514	        Fin.val_succ, Fin.coe_castPred]
515	    rw [pow_succ]; ring
516	
517	/-- **`d² = 0`** for the constant-coefficient alternating Čech complex, via the
518	sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)`. -/
519	lemma combDifferential_comp (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) :
520	    combDifferential (combDifferential t) = 0 := by
521	  funext σ
522	  simp only [combDifferential, Pi.zero_apply, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
523	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 1) =>
524	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) • t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))]
525	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
526	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
527	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
528	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
529	      funext k
530	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
531	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
532	    simp only [harg]
533	    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
534	    simp
535	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
536	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
537	    intro hj
538	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
539	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
540	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
541	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
542	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
543	
544	/-- **Positive-degree exactness** of the constant-coefficient Čech complex
545	(`Function.Exact` form).  Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential_exact`. -/
546	lemma combDifferential_exact (r : ι) (n : ℕ) :
547	    Function.Exact (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M))
548	      (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 3) → ι) → M)) := by
549	  intro x
550	  constructor
551	  · intro hx
552	    exact ⟨combHomotopy r x, combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle r x hx⟩
553	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
554	    exact combDifferential_comp y
555	
556	end FreeCechEngine
557	
558	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sectionwise reduction of the free Čech complex -/
559	
560	/-- **Evaluation preserves the degreewise coproduct of the free Čech complex.**
561	
562	The objectwise-reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation` requires understanding the evaluation of
563	`cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰` at an open `V`.  Since the degree-`p` term is the coproduct
564	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and the evaluation functor
565	preserves finite colimits (`PresheafOfModules.Finite.evaluation_preservesFiniteColimits`,
566	with `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` finite as `𝒰.I₀` is finite), evaluating commutes with the
567	coproduct:
568	`(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_p) ≅ ∐_σ (evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))`.
569	This is the degree-`p` entry point for the sectionwise description
570	(blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`); each evaluated summand
571	`(evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj W) = (ModuleCat.free (O_X(V))).obj (V ⟶ W)` is the free
572	`O_X(V)`-module on the (subsingleton) hom-set `V ⟶ W`, i.e. `O_X(V)` if `V ≤ W` and `0`
573	otherwise.
574	
575	Project-local: the assembly of evaluation-preserves-coproduct with the free Čech complex
576	has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
577	noncomputable def cechFreeEval_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
578	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (p : ℕ) :
579	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V).obj ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p)
580	      ≅ ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
581	          (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V).obj
582	            (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
583	  haveI : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete (Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀))
584	      (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V) := by
585	    haveI := PresheafOfModules.Finite.evaluation_preservesFiniteColimits X.ringCatSheaf.obj V
586	    infer_instance
587	  exact Limits.PreservesCoproduct.iso _ _
588	
589	/-- **Evaluating `freeYoneda W` at an open `V ⊄ W` gives the zero module.**
590	
591	`(evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj W) = (ModuleCat.free (O_X(V))).obj (V ⟶ W)` is the free
592	`O_X(V)`-module on the hom-set `V ⟶ W`.  When `V ≰ W` that hom-set is empty, so the module
593	is `0`.  This is the per-summand input to the empty case of the sectionwise reduction (and
594	kills the non-`I₁` summands in the nonempty case).
595	
596	Project-local: a degreewise vanishing statement for the project's `freeYoneda`. -/
597	lemma freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le {W V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : ¬ V ≤ W) :
598	    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
599	      (freeYoneda.obj W)) := by
600	  haveI : IsEmpty (V ⟶ W) := ⟨fun f => h (leOfHom f)⟩
601	  haveI : Subsingleton ↑((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj
602	      (Opposite.op V)).obj (freeYoneda.obj W)) :=
603	    ⟨fun a b => Finsupp.ext (fun x => (IsEmpty.false x).elim)⟩
604	  exact ModuleCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _
605	
606	/-- **Evaluating `freeYoneda W` at an open `V ≤ W` gives `O_X(V)`.**
607	
608	When `V ≤ W` the hom-set `V ⟶ W` is a singleton (`Unique`), so the free `O_X(V)`-module on it
609	is `O_X(V)` itself.  This is the per-summand identification of the surviving (`I₁`) summands in
610	the sectionwise reduction (blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`): together with
611	`freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le` it gives the description
612	`K(𝒰)_p(V) = ⊕_{σ : V ≤ U_σ} O_X(V)`.
613	
614	Project-local: a degreewise identification for the project's `freeYoneda`. -/
615	noncomputable def freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le {W V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ W) :
616	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj (freeYoneda.obj W)
617	      ≅ ModuleCat.of (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))
618	          (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
619	  haveI : Unique (V ⟶ W) := ⟨⟨homOfLE h⟩, fun _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _⟩
620	  (Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique _ _ (V ⟶ W)).toModuleIso
621	
622	/-- A coproduct of zero objects is a zero object: every coproduct injection out of a zero
623	object is zero, so the identity of the coproduct is zero. -/
624	lemma isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms C]
625	    {β : Type*} (f : β → C) [Limits.HasCoproduct f]
626	    (h : ∀ b, Limits.IsZero (f b)) : Limits.IsZero (∐ f) := by
627	  rw [Limits.IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero]
628	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
629	  intro b
630	  rw [Category.comp_id, Limits.comp_zero]
631	  exact (h b).eq_zero_of_src _
632	
633	/-- **Empty case of the sectionwise reduction (object level).**
634	
635	If no cover member `U_i` contains `V` (i.e. `I₁(V) = ∅`), then the degree-`p` term of the
636	free Čech complex evaluates to the zero module at `V`.  Indeed every multi-index intersection
637	`coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 (σ 0)`, so `V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` would force `V` into a
638	cover member, contradiction; hence each summand vanishes by `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`
639	and the coproduct is zero.  This is the object-level input to
640	`cech_free_eval_empty`. -/
641	lemma cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
642	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) (hV : ∀ i, ¬ V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) :
643	    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
644	      ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p)) := by
645	  refine Limits.IsZero.of_iso ?_ (cechFreeEval_X 𝒰 (Opposite.op V) p)
646	  apply isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero
647	  intro σ
648	  apply freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le
649	  intro hle
650	  exact hV (σ 0) (le_trans hle (iInf_le (fun k => coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)) 0))
651	
652	/-- **Empty case — the cover structure presheaf evaluates to zero.**
653	
654	If no cover member contains `V` (`I₁(V) = ∅`), then `O_𝒰(V) = 0`.  The cover structure
655	presheaf is the image of the augmentation `cechFreeAug`, so `O_𝒰` is an epimorphic image of
656	`K(𝒰)_0` via `factorThruImage`; evaluating at `V` (which preserves epimorphisms) gives an
657	epimorphism out of the zero module `(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_0)`
658	(`cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty`), so its target is zero. -/
659	lemma coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
660	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (hV : ∀ i, ¬ V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) :
661	    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
662	      (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰)) := by
663	  change Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
664	      (Limits.image (cechFreeAug 𝒰)))
665	  haveI : Epi ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
666	      (Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰))) :=
667	    Functor.map_epi (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V))

668	      (Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰))
669	  exact Limits.IsZero.of_epi
670	    ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
671	      (Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰)))
672	    (cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty 𝒰 V 0 hV)
673	
674	/-- The homology of a homological complex vanishes in any degree where the complex object
675	vanishes: the degree-`i` homology is a subquotient of `K.X i`. -/
676	lemma isZero_homology_of_isZero_X {C : Type*} [Category C] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms C]
677	    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} (K : HomologicalComplex C c) (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i]
678	    (h : Limits.IsZero (K.X i)) : Limits.IsZero (K.homology i) := by
679	  rw [← K.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology i, HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff,
680	    ShortComplex.exact_iff_isZero_homology]
681	  exact ShortComplex.isZero_homology_of_isZero_X₂ _ h
682	
683	/-- **Empty case of the sectionwise reduction** (`lem:cech_free_eval_empty`).
684	
685	If no cover member contains `V` (`I₁(V) = ∅`), the evaluation at `V` of the augmentation
686	chain map `cechFreeComplexAug` is a quasi-isomorphism: both the evaluated free complex
687	(`cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty`) and the evaluated target
688	(`coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty`) are objectwise zero, so all their homology
689	vanishes and the induced homology map is an isomorphism between zero objects. -/
690	lemma cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
691	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (hV : ∀ i, ¬ V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) :
692	    QuasiIso (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj
693	      (Opposite.op V)).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.down ℕ)).map
694	        (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰)) := by
695	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
696	  intro i
697	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
698	  refine Limits.isIso_of_source_target_iso_zero _
699	    (Limits.IsZero.isoZero ?_) (Limits.IsZero.isoZero ?_)
700	  · exact isZero_homology_of_isZero_X _ i (cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty 𝒰 V i hV)
701	  · apply isZero_homology_of_isZero_X
702	    rcases i with _ | n
703	    · exact coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty 𝒰 V hV
704	    · exact Limits.IsZero.of_iso (Limits.isZero_zero _)
705	        ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).mapZeroObject)
