707	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — degreewise engine model of the evaluated free complex
708	
709	The nonempty-case analysis of the evaluated free Čech complex needs an explicit degreewise
710	identification of `(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_p)` with the constant-coefficient combinatorial model
711	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)`, where `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`.  This section builds that
712	degreewise object isomorphism as the composite
713	
714	  `(eval V)(K_p) ≅[cechFreeEval_X] ∐_{σ : Fin(p+1)→I₀} (eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)`
715	  `           ≅[drop-zeros]      ∐_{σ : V ≤ U_σ} (eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)`
716	  `           ≅[whiskerEquiv]    ∐_{τ : Fin(p+1)→I₁(V)} O_X(V),`
717	
718	using `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le` to discard the summands with `V ≰ U_σ` and
719	`freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le` to identify each surviving summand with `O_X(V)`.  The differential
720	match turning this degreewise iso into an iso of chain complexes is the genuine remaining
721	bottleneck and is NOT discharged here. -/
722	
723	/-- `V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` holds iff every value of `σ` indexes a cover member containing `V`;
724	i.e. `σ` factors through `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`.  This is the index-splitting criterion separating
725	the surviving summands of the evaluated free Čech complex from the vanishing ones. -/
726	lemma le_coverInterOpen_iff (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
727	    {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
728	    V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ↔ ∀ k, V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) := by
729	  simp only [coverInterOpen, le_iInf_iff]
730	
731	/-- **Reindexing the surviving multi-indices.** The multi-indices `σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` with
732	`V ≤ U_σ` are in bijection with the maps `Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)` into the subtype
733	`I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` of cover members containing `V`.  This bijection feeds
734	`Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv` to collapse the
735	surviving coproduct onto the constant-coefficient combinatorial index `Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)`. -/
736	def survivingEquiv (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
737	    (Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) ≃
738	      {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} where
739	  toFun τ := ⟨fun k => (τ k).1, (le_coverInterOpen_iff 𝒰 V _).2 fun k => (τ k).2⟩
740	  invFun σ := fun k => ⟨σ.1 k, (le_coverInterOpen_iff 𝒰 V σ.1).1 σ.2 k⟩
741	  left_inv τ := by funext k; rfl
742	  right_inv σ := by ext k; rfl
743	
744	/-- **Drop-zeros isomorphism.** Evaluating the degree-`p` free Čech term at `V` and splitting off
745	the vanishing summands: the full coproduct over all `σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` is isomorphic to the
746	coproduct over the *surviving* multi-indices `{σ : V ≤ U_σ}`, because every non-surviving summand
747	`(eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)` (with `V ≰ U_σ`) is a zero object
748	(`freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`).  This is the first of the two halves identifying the evaluated
749	term with the combinatorial model. -/
750	noncomputable def cechFreeEvalDropZeros (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
751	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
752	    (∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
753	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
754	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))) ≅
755	      (∐ fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} =>
756	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
757	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 s.1))) where
758	  hom := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ => by
759	    classical
760	    exact if h : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ then
761	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} =>
762	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
763	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 s.1))) ⟨σ, h⟩
764	    else 0
765	  inv := Limits.Sigma.desc fun s =>
766	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
767	      (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
768	        (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))) s.1
769	  hom_inv_id := by
770	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
771	    rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.comp_id]
772	    by_cases h : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ
773	    · rw [dif_pos h, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
774	    · rw [dif_neg h, Limits.zero_comp]
775	      exact ((freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le h).eq_zero_of_src _).symm
776	  inv_hom_id := by
777	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro s
778	    rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.comp_id, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, dif_pos s.2]
779	
780	/-- **Degreewise engine model of the evaluated free Čech term.** The degree-`p` term of the
781	evaluated free Čech complex at `V` is isomorphic to the constant-coefficient combinatorial model
782	`∐_{τ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)`, where `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`.  Built as the composite of
783	`cechFreeEval_X` (evaluation commutes with the coproduct), `cechFreeEvalDropZeros` (discard the
784	`V ≰ U_σ` summands), and `Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv` along `survivingEquiv` with the per-summand
785	identification `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le`.  This is the object half of the engine iso
786	`cechFreeEvalEngineIso`; the differential match remains. -/
787	noncomputable def cechFreeEvalEngine_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
788	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
789	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
790	        ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p) ≅
791	      ∐ fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} =>
792	        ModuleCat.of (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))
793	          (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
794	  cechFreeEval_X 𝒰 (Opposite.op V) p ≪≫ cechFreeEvalDropZeros 𝒰 V p ≪≫
795	    (Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv (survivingEquiv 𝒰 V p)
796	      (g := fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} =>
797	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
798	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 s.1)))
799	      (fun τ => freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le ((survivingEquiv 𝒰 V p τ).2))).symm
800	
801	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the engine chain complex `C•`
802	
803	The degreewise model `∐_{τ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)` assembles into an honest chain complex of
804	`O_X(V)`-modules, the **engine complex** `cechEngineComplex`.  Its differential `C_{p+1} ⟶ C_p` is
805	the alternating sum of the index-dropping coproduct reindexings `σ ↦ σ ∘ Fin.succAbove i` — this is
806	the
807	chain (insertion) form of the Stacks `lemma-homology-complex` differential, the chain dual of the
808	constant-coefficient `FreeCechEngine.combDifferential`.  `cechFreeEvalEngineIso` (next) identifies
809	the evaluated free Čech complex with `cechEngineComplex`; this section provides its target. -/
810	
811	/-- The constant coefficient module `O_X(V)` viewed as a module over itself.  This is the
812	per-summand target of `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le`, hence the coefficient of the engine complex. -/
813	abbrev coverSectionModule (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
814	    ModuleCat (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
815	  ModuleCat.of _ (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))
816	
817	/-- The degree-`p` object of the engine complex: `∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)`. -/
818	noncomputable abbrev cechEngineX (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
819	    ModuleCat (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
820	  ∐ fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} => coverSectionModule V
821	
822	/-- The engine differential `C_{p+1} ⟶ C_p`: the alternating sum over `i : Fin (p+2)` of the
823	coproduct reindexing maps `ι_σ ↦ (-1)^i • ι_{σ ∘ Fin.succAbove i}` that drop the `i`-th index of
824	the multi-index `σ : Fin (p+2) → I₁(V)`.  Chain (insertion) dual of
825	`FreeCechEngine.combDifferential`. -/
826	noncomputable def cechEngineD (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
827	    cechEngineX 𝒰 V (p + 1) ⟶ cechEngineX 𝒰 V p :=
828	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ => ∑ i : Fin (p + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
829	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} => coverSectionModule V)
830	      (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
831	
832	/-- Action of the engine differential on a coproduct injection: `ι_σ ≫ cechEngineD = ∑_i (-1)^i •
833	ι_{σ ∘ succAbove i}`. -/
834	lemma cechEngineD_ι (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ)
835	    (σ : Fin (p + 2) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) :
836	    Limits.Sigma.ι _ σ ≫ cechEngineD 𝒰 V p
837	      = ∑ i : Fin (p + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
838	          Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} =>
839	            coverSectionModule V) (σ ∘ i.succAbove) := by
840	  simp only [cechEngineD, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
841	
842	/-- **`d² = 0` for the engine complex**, via the same sign-reversing involution
843	`(i, j) ↦ (i.succAbove j, j.predAbove i)` as `FreeCechEngine.combDifferential_comp`, transported to
844	the coproduct injections. -/
845	lemma cechEngineD_comp (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
846	    cechEngineD 𝒰 V (p + 1) ≫ cechEngineD 𝒰 V p = 0 := by
847	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
848	  rw [Limits.comp_zero, ← Category.assoc, cechEngineD_ι]
849	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
850	  simp only [Preadditive.zsmul_comp, cechEngineD_ι, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
851	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun q : Fin (p + 3) × Fin (p + 2) =>
852	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (q.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (q.2 : ℕ)) •
853	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} =>
854	        coverSectionModule V) ((σ ∘ q.1.succAbove) ∘ q.2.succAbove))]
855	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun q _ => (q.1.succAbove q.2, q.2.predAbove q.1))
856	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
857	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
858	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
859	      funext k
860	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
861	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
862	    simp only [harg]
863	    rw [← add_smul, FreeCechEngine.combSign_flip j i]
864	    simp
865	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
866	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
867	    intro hj
868	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
869	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
870	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
871	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
872	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
873	
874	/-- **The engine chain complex** `C•` of an open `V` meeting some cover member.
875	
876	The chain complex of `O_X(V)`-modules with `C_p = ∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)` and the
877	alternating index-dropping differential `cechEngineD`.  It is the constant-coefficient combinatorial
878	model that `cechFreeEvalEngineIso` identifies with the evaluated free Čech complex; the nonempty
879	acyclicity of the evaluated complex reduces to the contractibility of `C•`.
880	
881	Project-local: the constant-coefficient Čech model complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
882	noncomputable def cechEngineComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
883	    ChainComplex (ModuleCat (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))) ℕ :=
884	  ChainComplex.of (cechEngineX 𝒰 V) (cechEngineD 𝒰 V) (cechEngineD_comp 𝒰 V)
885	
886	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — contracting homotopy of the engine complex
887	
888	Fixing an index `i_fix ∈ I₁(V)` (which exists exactly when `V` meets some cover member), the
889	prepend-`i_fix` map `ι_σ ↦ ι_{Fin.cons i_fix σ}` contracts the engine complex: `d ∘ s + s ∘ d = id`
890	in every positive degree.  This is the chain (coproduct) port of `FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy` /
891	`combHomotopy_spec`, and it yields the positive-degree exactness of `cechEngineComplex` feeding the
892	nonempty case of the free Čech quasi-isomorphism. -/
893	
894	/-- The prepend-`i_fix` contracting map `C_p ⟶ C_{p+1}` of the engine complex: on the coproduct
895	injection `ι_σ` (`σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)`) it returns `ι_{Fin.cons i_fix σ}`. -/
896	noncomputable def cechEnginePrepend (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
897	    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) (p : ℕ) :
898	    cechEngineX 𝒰 V p ⟶ cechEngineX 𝒰 V (p + 1) :=
899	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
900	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} => coverSectionModule V)
901	      (Fin.cons i_fix σ)
902	
903	/-- Action of the prepend map on a coproduct injection. -/
904	lemma cechEnginePrepend_ι (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
905	    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) (p : ℕ)
906	    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) :
907	    Limits.Sigma.ι _ σ ≫ cechEnginePrepend 𝒰 V i_fix p
908	      = Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} =>
909	          coverSectionModule V) (Fin.cons i_fix σ) := by
910	  simp only [cechEnginePrepend, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
911	
912	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** `d ∘ s + s ∘ d = id` for the engine complex in positive
913	degree: the coproduct port of `FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy_spec`.  The `i = 0` term of `s ≫ d`
914	returns the identity (dropping the just-prepended `i_fix`), and every higher term cancels in pairs
915	against the corresponding term of `d ≫ s` by `FreeCechEngine.cons_comp_succAbove_succ`. -/
916	lemma cechEnginePrepend_spec (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
917	    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) (n : ℕ) :
918	    cechEnginePrepend 𝒰 V i_fix (n + 1) ≫ cechEngineD 𝒰 V (n + 1)
919	      + cechEngineD 𝒰 V n ≫ cechEnginePrepend 𝒰 V i_fix n
920	      = 𝟙 (cechEngineX 𝒰 V (n + 1)) := by
921	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
922	  rw [Preadditive.comp_add, Category.comp_id]
923	  rw [← Category.assoc, cechEnginePrepend_ι, cechEngineD_ι]
924	  rw [← Category.assoc, cechEngineD_ι, Preadditive.sum_comp]
925	  simp only [Preadditive.zsmul_comp, cechEnginePrepend_ι]
926	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun i : Fin (n + 3) => (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
927	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (n + 2) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} => coverSectionModule V)
928	      ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (n + 3) → _) ∘ i.succAbove))]
929	  have h0 : (Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (n + 3) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i})
930	      ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 3)).succAbove = σ := by funext k; simp
931	  rw [h0]
932	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
933	  rw [add_assoc, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
934	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun k _ => by
935	    rw [FreeCechEngine.cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]
936	    abel), add_zero]
937	
938	/-- **Positive-degree exactness of the engine complex** (`Function.Exact` form), the coproduct port
939	of `FreeCechEngine.combDifferential_exact`.  When `I₁(V)` is nonempty (witnessed by `i_fix`), the
940	engine differential is exact at every positive degree: a cycle `x` with `cechEngineD n x = 0` is the
941	boundary `cechEngineD (n+1) (cechEnginePrepend (n+1) x)`, by the contracting identity
942	`cechEnginePrepend_spec`.  Feeds the nonempty-case homology vanishing via
943	`ModuleCat.shortComplex_exact`. -/
944	lemma cechEngineD_exact (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
945	    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) (n : ℕ) :
946	    Function.Exact (cechEngineD 𝒰 V (n + 1)) (cechEngineD 𝒰 V n) := by
947	  intro x
948	  constructor
949	  · intro hx
950	    refine ⟨(cechEnginePrepend 𝒰 V i_fix (n + 1)) x, ?_⟩
951	    have hspec := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (cechEnginePrepend_spec 𝒰 V i_fix n)
952	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add, ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_id] at hspec
953	    have hx2 := DFunLike.congr_fun hspec x
954	    rw [LinearMap.add_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.id_apply,
955	      hx, map_zero, add_zero] at hx2
956	    exact hx2
957	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
958	    have hcomp := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (cechEngineD_comp 𝒰 V n)
959	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_zero] at hcomp
960	    have h2 := DFunLike.congr_fun hcomp y
961	    rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.zero_apply] at h2
962	    exact h2

964	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — naturality bridge for the engine differential match
965	
966	The differential comm-square of `cechFreeEvalEngineIso` reduces, summand-by-summand, to the
967	naturality of the per-summand identification `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le`.  The key observation is
968	that this identification iso is *exactly* the evaluation of the augmentation `freeYonedaAug`: both
969	are the `O_X(V)`-linear map out of the free module on the singleton hom-set `V ⟶ W` sending the
970	generator to `1`.  Hence the naturality of `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le` is the already-proven
971	`freeYoneda_map_comp_aug`, evaluated at `V`. -/
972	
973	/-- The identification iso `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le h` (for `V ≤ W`) coincides with the
974	evaluation of the augmentation `freeYonedaAug W` at `V`.  Both are the unique `O_X(V)`-linear map
975	from the free module on the singleton hom-set `V ⟶ W` sending the generator `freeMk g` to
976	`1 ∈ O_X(V)`. -/
977	lemma freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le_hom_eq_aug {W V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ W) :
978	    (freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le h).hom = (freeYonedaAug W).app (Opposite.op V) := by
979	  haveI : Unique (V ⟶ W) := ⟨⟨homOfLE h⟩, fun _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _⟩
980	  apply ModuleCat.free_hom_ext
981	  intro g
982	  have hL : (ConcreteCategory.hom (freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le h).hom) (ModuleCat.freeMk g)
983	      = (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
984	    simp only [freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le, LinearEquiv.toModuleIso_hom]
985	    change (Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique _ _ (V ⟶ W)) (ModuleCat.freeMk g) = 1
986	    rw [Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique_apply]
987	    simp only [ModuleCat.freeMk, Unique.eq_default g, Finsupp.single_eq_same]
988	  exact hL.trans (freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk (g : V ⟶ W)).symm
989	
990	/-- **Naturality of the per-summand identification `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le`.** For
991	`A ≤ B` (representable index-dropping inclusion) and `V ≤ A`, evaluating the free-presheaf
992	restriction `freeYoneda.map (homOfLE h₁) : freeYoneda A ⟶ freeYoneda B` at `V` and then
993	identifying with `O_X(V)` is the same as identifying directly: the restriction acts as the
994	identity on the constant coefficient `O_X(V)`.  This is the summand-for-summand input to the
995	differential comm-square of `cechFreeEvalEngineIso`, obtained from the augmentation naturality
996	`freeYoneda_map_comp_aug` via the bridge `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le_hom_eq_aug`. -/
997	lemma freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le_natural {A B V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h₁ : A ≤ B)
998	    (hVA : V ≤ A) :
999	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
1000	        (freeYoneda.map (homOfLE h₁)) ≫ (freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le (le_trans hVA h₁)).hom
1001	      = (freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le hVA).hom := by
1002	  rw [freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le_hom_eq_aug, freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le_hom_eq_aug,
1003	    PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map, ← freeYoneda_map_comp_aug h₁, PresheafOfModules.comp_app]
1004	  rfl
1005	
1006	/-- Action of `(cechFreeEval_X).inv` on a coproduct injection: it is `(eval V)` applied to the
1007	corresponding injection of the unevaluated free Čech term.  This is the `PreservesCoproduct.iso`
1008	naturality (`ι_comp_sigmaComparison`) specialised to `cechFreeEval_X`. -/
1009	private lemma cechFreeEval_X_ι_inv (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
1010	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) (σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
1011	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
1012	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
1013	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))) σ ≫ (cechFreeEval_X 𝒰 (Opposite.op V) p).inv
1014	      = (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
1015	          (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
1016	            freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) σ) := by
1017	  have hinv : (cechFreeEval_X 𝒰 (Opposite.op V) p).inv
1018	      = Limits.sigmaComparison (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V))
1019	          (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := rfl
1020	  rw [hinv]
1021	  exact Limits.ι_comp_sigmaComparison _ _ σ

1023	/-- **Reduced action of the engine identification on a surviving injection.** For a multi-index
1024	`σ` with `V ≤ U_σ`, transporting the `σ`-injection through `(cechFreeEval_X).inv` and the engine

1026	`k ↦ ⟨σ k, _⟩ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)`, precomposed by the augmentation `freeYonedaAug` at `V`.

1028	private lemma cechFreeEvalEngine_X_inv_hom_ι (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
1029	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) (σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)
1030	    (hσ : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) :
1031	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
1032	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj

1037	            coverSectionModule V) (fun k => ⟨σ k, (le_coverInterOpen_iff 𝒰 V σ).1 hσ k⟩) := by
1038	  rw [cechFreeEvalEngine_X]

1040	  rw [← Category.assoc]
1041	  simp only [cechFreeEvalDropZeros, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, dif_pos hσ, Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv,

1060	        ≫ (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d (p + 1) p

1089	private lemma cechFreeEvalEngine_map_ι (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
1090	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) (τ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)
1091	    (hτ : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ) :
1092	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
1093	        (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) τ)
1094	      ≫ (cechFreeEvalEngine_X 𝒰 V p).hom
1095	      = (freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le hτ).hom
1096	        ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} =>
1097	            coverSectionModule V) (fun k => ⟨τ k, (le_coverInterOpen_iff 𝒰 V τ).1 hτ k⟩) := by
1098	  rw [← cechFreeEval_X_ι_inv 𝒰 V p τ]
1099	  erw [Category.assoc, cechFreeEvalEngine_X_inv_hom_ι 𝒰 V p τ hτ]
1100	
1101	/-- **Differential comm-square of the engine identification.** The degreewise object isos
1102	`cechFreeEvalEngine_X` intertwine the evaluated free Čech differential with the engine
1103	differential `cechEngineD`.  This is the single comm-square upgrading the degreewise object iso to
1104	the chain iso `cechFreeEvalEngineIso`. -/
1105	private lemma cechFreeEvalEngine_comm (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
1106	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
1107	    (cechFreeEvalEngine_X 𝒰 V (p + 1)).hom ≫ cechEngineD 𝒰 V p
1108	      = (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
1109	          ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d (p + 1) p) ≫ (cechFreeEvalEngine_X 𝒰 V p).hom := by
1110	  refine (cancel_epi (cechFreeEval_X 𝒰 (Opposite.op V) (p + 1)).inv).mp ?_
1111	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
1112	  intro σ
1113	  by_cases hσ : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ
1114	  · slice_lhs 1 3 => rw [cechFreeEvalEngine_X_inv_hom_ι 𝒰 V (p + 1) σ hσ]
1115	    slice_lhs 2 3 => rw [cechEngineD_ι]
1116	    slice_rhs 1 2 => rw [cechFreeEval_X_ι_inv 𝒰 V (p + 1) σ]
1117	    erw [← Functor.map_comp, cechFree_d_ι 𝒰 p σ]
1118	    erw [Functor.map_sum]
1119	    rw [Preadditive.comp_sum]
1120	    erw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
1121	    apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
1122	    intro i _
1123	    erw [Preadditive.comp_zsmul, Functor.map_zsmul, Preadditive.zsmul_comp]
1124	    congr 1
1125	    have hσi : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ i.succAbove) :=
1126	      le_trans hσ (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 i.succAbove σ)
1127	    erw [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc, cechFreeEvalEngine_map_ι 𝒰 V p (σ ∘ i.succAbove) hσi]
1128	    erw [← Category.assoc,
1129	      freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le_natural (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 i.succAbove σ) hσ]
1130	    rfl
1131	  · exact (freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le hσ).eq_of_src _ _
1132	
1133	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the engine chain isomorphism
1134	
1135	The differential comm-square `cechFreeEvalEngine_comm` upgrades the degreewise object isos
1136	`cechFreeEvalEngine_X` to an isomorphism of chain complexes between the evaluation of the free Čech
1137	complex at `V` and the constant-coefficient engine complex `cechEngineComplex`.  This is the chain
1138	iso the nonempty-case quasi-isomorphism is transferred across. -/
1139	
1140	/-- **The engine chain isomorphism** (`lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso`).  For each open `V`, the
1141	evaluation at `V` of the free Čech complex is isomorphic, as a chain complex, to the
1142	constant-coefficient engine complex `cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V`.  Built from the degreewise object isos
1143	`cechFreeEvalEngine_X` and the differential comm-square `cechFreeEvalEngine_comm`.
1144	
1145	Project-local: the identification of the evaluated free Čech complex with its combinatorial
1146	model has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
1147	noncomputable def cechFreeEvalEngineIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
1148	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
1149	    ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).mapHomologicalComplex
1150	        (ComplexShape.down ℕ)).obj (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰) ≅ cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V :=
1151	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun p => cechFreeEvalEngine_X 𝒰 V p) (by
1152	    intro i j hij
1153	    rw [ComplexShape.down_Rel] at hij
1154	    obtain rfl : i = j + 1 := hij.symm
1155	    rw [Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d,
1156	      show (cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V).d (j + 1) j = cechEngineD 𝒰 V j from ChainComplex.of_d _ _ _ j]
1157	    exact cechFreeEvalEngine_comm 𝒰 V j)
1158	
1159	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — positive-degree acyclicity of the engine complex
1160	
1161	When `I₁(V) ≠ ∅` (witnessed by `i_fix`), the engine complex is exact in every positive degree.
1162	This is the homology-vanishing input (transported across `cechFreeEvalEngineIso`) to the nonempty
1163	case of the free Čech quasi-isomorphism. -/
1164	
1165	/-- **Positive-degree exactness of the engine complex.** When `I₁(V)` is nonempty (witnessed by
1166	`i_fix`), `cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V` is exact at every positive degree `n + 1`, from the
1167	contracting-homotopy exactness `cechEngineD_exact` via `ModuleCat.shortComplex_exact`. -/
1168	lemma cechEngineComplex_exactAt (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
1169	    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) (n : ℕ) :
1170	    (cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V).ExactAt (n + 1) := by
1171	  rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff' _ (n + 2) (n + 1) n (by simp [ChainComplex.prev])
1172	    (by simp)]
1173	  apply ModuleCat.shortComplex_exact
1174	  have hf : (cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V).d (n + 2) (n + 1) = cechEngineD 𝒰 V (n + 1) :=
1175	    ChainComplex.of_d _ _ _ (n + 1)
1176	  have hg : (cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V).d (n + 1) n = cechEngineD 𝒰 V n := ChainComplex.of_d _ _ _ n
1177	  change Function.Exact ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V).d (n + 2) (n + 1)))
1178	    ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V).d (n + 1) n))
1179	  rw [hf, hg]
1180	  exact cechEngineD_exact 𝒰 V i_fix n
1181	
1182	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — augmentation of the engine complex
1183	
1184	The engine complex augments onto its degree-`0` coefficient `O_X(V)` via the codiagonal of the
1185	constant summands.  Together with the positive-degree acyclicity `cechEngineComplex_exactAt` and the
1186	degree-`0` contracting identity this exhibits `cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V` as a resolution of `O_X(V)`
1187	in the nonempty case — the engine-side input to `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty`. -/
1188	
1189	/-- **Degree-`0` augmentation of the engine complex**: the codiagonal `∐_{σ : Fin 1 → I₁(V)} O_X(V)
1190	⟶ O_X(V)` sending every summand by the identity. -/
1191	noncomputable def cechEngineAug0 (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
1192	    cechEngineX 𝒰 V 0 ⟶ coverSectionModule V :=
1193	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun _ => 𝟙 (coverSectionModule V)
1194	
1195	/-- Action of the degree-`0` engine augmentation on an injection: `ι_σ ≫ aug = 𝟙`. -/
1196	lemma cechEngineAug0_ι (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
1197	    (σ : Fin 1 → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) :
1198	    Limits.Sigma.ι _ σ ≫ cechEngineAug0 𝒰 V = 𝟙 (coverSectionModule V) := by
1199	  simp only [cechEngineAug0, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
1200	
1201	/-- The degree-`0` engine differential composed with the augmentation vanishes: the alternating
1202	face sum `δ₀ − δ₁` is killed because both faces become the identity after augmenting.  The
1203	cochain-map condition for the engine augmentation `cechEngineComplexAug`. -/
1204	lemma cechEngineD_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
1205	    cechEngineD 𝒰 V 0 ≫ cechEngineAug0 𝒰 V = 0 := by
1206	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
1207	  intro σ
1208	  rw [Limits.comp_zero, ← Category.assoc, cechEngineD_ι, Preadditive.sum_comp, Fin.sum_univ_two]
1209	  simp only [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]
1210	  erw [cechEngineAug0_ι, cechEngineAug0_ι]
1211	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
1212	  abel
1213	
1214	/-- **The augmentation chain map** `cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V ⟶ O_X(V)[0]`, whose degree-`0` component
1215	is the codiagonal `cechEngineAug0`.  The chain-map condition is `cechEngineD_comp_aug`. -/
1216	noncomputable def cechEngineComplexAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
1217	    cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V ⟶ (ChainComplex.single₀ _).obj (coverSectionModule V) :=
1218	  ((cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V).toSingle₀Equiv (coverSectionModule V)).symm
1219	    ⟨cechEngineAug0 𝒰 V, by
1220	      rw [show (cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V).d 1 0 = cechEngineD 𝒰 V 0 from ChainComplex.of_d _ _ _ 0]
1221	      exact cechEngineD_comp_aug 𝒰 V⟩
1222	
1223	end AlgebraicGeometry
1224

2200	  combinatorial content of \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy}\) of
2201	  Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}, here with the single
2202	  coefficient module \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), transported across the degreewise
2203	  identification of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso}. The maps \(h_p\) (together with the
2204	  degree \((-1)\!\to\!0\) map onto \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\))
2205	  are the homotopy hom maps assembled into a
2206	  \(\operatorname{HomologicalComplex.Homotopy}\) in
2207	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty}.
2208	\end{lemma}
2209	
2210	\begin{proof}
2211	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}
2212	  Under the identification of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise} each
2213	  evaluated term is a finite direct sum of copies of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) indexed by
2214	  \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\). The prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) prescription
2215	  sends the basis component at \((i_1, \ldots, i_{p+1})\) to the component at
2216	  \((i_{\mathrm{fix}}, i_1, \ldots, i_{p+1})\) and is \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-linear; it is
2217	  packaged as the \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\) of these per-summand maps, exactly as
2218	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy}\) is defined. This is a definition,
2219	  so there is nothing to prove beyond well-definedness, which is the linearity of
2220	  \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\).
2221	\end{proof}
2222	
2223	\begin{lemma}
2224	[The prepend homotopy contracts the augmented evaluated complex, by transport]
2225	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec}
2226	  % NOTE: the contracting identity `d ∘ s + s ∘ d = id` is established at the
2227	  % engine-complex level as `cechEnginePrepend_spec` (with positive-degree exactness
2228	  % packaged as `cechEngineD_exact`), both pinned by lem:cech_engine_complex. This
2229	  % block carries no standalone `\lean{}` pin: the evaluated-complex identity follows
2230	  % by transporting `cechEnginePrepend_spec` across `cechFreeEvalEngineIso` of
2231	  % lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso.
2232	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:free_cech_engine, lem:cech_engine_complex, lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso}
2233	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2234	  % (proof, the $dh + hd = \mathrm{id}$ computation) (read from
2235	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1272--1285).
2236	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Then we compute
2237	  % $(dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_0 \ldots i_p} +
2238	  % \sum_{j \geq 1}\sum_{i \in I_1}
2239	  % (-1)^j (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) s_{i_1 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}
2240	  % + (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) d(s)_{i_1 \ldots i_p}$
2241	  % which is equal to $s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ as desired."
2242	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2243	  (the contracting identity).}
2244	  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy}
2245	  (\(I_1(V) \neq \varnothing\), \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} \in I_1\) fixed), the transported
2246	  prepend homotopy \(h\) satisfies the contracting identity
2247	  \[
2248	    d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}
2249	  \]
2250	  on the augmented evaluated complex
2251	  \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1(V) \to K(\mathcal{U})_0(V) \to
2252	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) \to 0\) (including the degree-\(0\)/degree-\((-1)\)
2253	  augmentation term). Here \(d\) is the alternating-sum {\v C}ech differential of
2254	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}.
2255	\end{lemma}
2256	
2257	\begin{proof}
2258	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:free_cech_engine}
2259	  This is the same alternating-sum cancellation as
2260	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy\_spec}\)
2261	  (\(\operatorname{combDifferential}(\operatorname{combHomotopy} r\, t) +
2262	  \operatorname{combHomotopy} r\, (\operatorname{combDifferential} t) = t\)),
2263	  specialised to coefficients \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\). Expanding
2264	  \((dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\): the \(j = 0\) term of \(dh\) prepends and then
2265	  removes \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), returning \(s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\); every remaining term
2266	  of \(dh\) cancels in pairs against the corresponding term of \(hd\) (the
2267	  prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) of the differential), because removing an index
2268	  \(j \geq 1\) after prepending \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) equals prepending
2269	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) after removing index \(j-1\), with opposite signs. The net
2270	  result is \(s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\), i.e.\ \(dh + hd = \mathrm{id}\); the index
2271	  bookkeeping is the \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.cons\_comp\_succAbove\_succ}\) /
2272	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combSign\_flip}\) content of
2273	  Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}.
2274	\end{proof}
2275	
2276	\begin{lemma}
2277	[Evaluated free complex over an open meeting a cover member]
2278	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty}
2279	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty}
2280	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise, lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso,
2281	    lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec,
2282	    lem:cech_engine_complex}
2283	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2284	  % (proof, homotopy-to-zero of the extended complex) (read from
2285	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1224--1245).
2286	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In fact, we claim that for every $W \subset X$ the complex
2287	  % $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$ is homotopy equivalent to the zero complex."
2288	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2289	  (homotopy equivalence to zero).}
2290	  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}, suppose
2291	  \(I_1(V) \neq \varnothing\). Then the evaluation at \(V\) of
2292	  \(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\) is a quasi-isomorphism: the augmented
2293	  evaluated complex is contractible, hence its homology is concentrated in degree
2294	  \(0\) where it equals \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\).
2295	\end{lemma}
2296	
2297	\begin{proof}
2298	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec}
2299	  By Lemmas~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy}
2300	  and~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec} the degreewise prepend maps
2301	  \(h_p\) satisfy \(dh + hd = \mathrm{id}\) on the augmented evaluated complex, so they
2302	  assemble into a \(\operatorname{HomologicalComplex.Homotopy}\) between the identity
2303	  and the zero endomorphism of that complex. A complex whose identity is homotopic to
2304	  zero is contractible; equivalently the augmented evaluated complex is a
2305	  \(\operatorname{HomotopyEquiv}\) of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet(V)\) with
2306	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V)[0] = \mathcal{O}_X(V)[0]\). A homotopy equivalence is a
2307	  quasi-isomorphism (\(\operatorname{Homotopy.toQuasiIso}\), or
2308	  \(\operatorname{HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso}\) /
2309	  \(\operatorname{QuasiIso.ofHomotopyEquiv}\)), so the evaluated augmentation map is a
2310	  quasi-isomorphism.
2311	\end{proof}
2312	
2313	\begin{lemma}
2314	[The free complex resolves \(\mathcal{O}_U\)]
2315	  \label{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}
2316	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeComplex_quasiIso}
2317	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf,
2318	    lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation, lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise,
2319	    lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso, lem:cech_free_eval_empty, lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty}
2320	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2321	  % (statement + proof) (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex,
2322	  % L1198--1284).
2323	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U} \subset \mathcal{O}_X$
2324	  % be the image presheaf of the map
2325	  % $\bigoplus j_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_i} \to \mathcal{O}_X$.
2326	  % The chain complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$ of presheaves
2327	  % of Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-map-into} above has homology presheaves
2328	  % $$
2329	  % H_i(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet) =
2330	  % \left\{
2331	  % \begin{matrix}
2332	  % 0 & \text{if} & i \not = 0 \\
2333	  % \mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U} & \text{if} & i = 0
2334	  % \end{matrix}
2335	  % \right.
2336	  % $$"
2337	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
2338	  \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}.}
2339	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
2340	  open covering. Let \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) be the cover structure presheaf of
2341	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_structure_presheaf}, the image presheaf of the
2342	  augmentation \(\bigoplus_i (j_i)_{!}\,\mathcal{O}_X|_{U_i} \to \mathcal{O}_X\) (so
2343	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when \(W\) is contained in some
2344	  \(U_i\), and \(0\) otherwise). Then the homology
2345	  presheaves of the free presheaf complex
2346	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
2347	  vanish away from degree \(0\) and equal \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) in degree
2348	  \(0\):
2349	  \[
2350	    H_i\bigl(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\bigr) =
2351	    \begin{cases}
2352	      \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} & i = 0, \\
2353	      0 & i \neq 0.
2354	    \end{cases}
2355	  \]
2356	  Equivalently, the augmented complex \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1 \to
2357	  K(\mathcal{U})_0 \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} \to 0\) is exact as a complex of
2358	  presheaves; that is, \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) is a (projective) resolution of
2359	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\), so the augmentation
2360	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\) is a
2361	  quasi-isomorphism — \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) is quasi-isomorphic to
2362	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) concentrated in degree \(0\).
2363	\end{lemma}
2364	
2365	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Consider the extended complex $K^{ext}_\bullet$ one gets by
2366	% putting
2367	% $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}$ in degree $-1$ with the obvious map
2368	% $K(\mathcal{U})_0 =
2369	% \bigoplus_{i_0} (j_{i_0})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0}} \to
2370	% \mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}$.
2371	% It suffices to show that taking sections of this extended complex over
2372	% any open $W \subset X$ leads to an acyclic complex.
2373	% In fact, we claim that for every $W \subset X$ the complex
2374	% $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$ is homotopy equivalent to the zero complex.
2375	% Write $I = I_1 \amalg I_2$ where $W \subset U_i$ if and only
2376	% if $i \in I_1$.
2377	% ...
2378	% If $I_1 \not = \emptyset$, then
2379	% $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)$
2380	% ...
2381	% Fix an element $i_{\text{fix}} \in I_1$. Define a map
2382	% $h : K^{ext}_p(W) \longrightarrow K^{ext}_{p + 1}(W)$
2383	% by the rule
2384	% $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} = (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) s_{i_1 \ldots i_p}$
2385	% ... which is equal to $s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ as desired."
2386	\begin{proof}
2387	  \uses{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation, lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise,
2388	    lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso, lem:cech_free_eval_empty, lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty}
2389	  The claim is that the augmentation chain map
2390	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\)
2391	  (\(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\)) is a quasi-isomorphism. By the
2392	  objectwise-reduction Lemma~\ref{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation}
2393	  (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_of\_evaluation}\)) it suffices to show that for every open
2394	  \(V \subset X\) the evaluation
2395	  \(\bigl((\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\, V).
2396	  \operatorname{mapHomologicalComplex}\bigr)(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug})\) is a
2397	  quasi-isomorphism of complexes of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-modules.
2398	
2399	  Fix \(V\) and set \(I_1 = I_1(V) = \{\,i : V \leq U_i\,\}\). By
2400	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise} the evaluated augmented complex is the
2401	  augmented combinatorial {\v C}ech complex of the simplex on \(I_1\) with
2402	  coefficients \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), with augmentation target
2403	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V)\). Split into two cases on \(I_1\). If
2404	  \(I_1 = \varnothing\), Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_empty} shows both sides vanish
2405	  and the evaluated augmentation is a quasi-isomorphism. If \(I_1 \neq \varnothing\),
2406	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty} provides the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)
2407	  contracting homotopy, exhibiting the augmented evaluated complex as contractible and
2408	  hence the evaluated augmentation as a quasi-isomorphism. In either case the
2409	  hypothesis of Lemma~\ref{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation} holds for \(V\), so
2410	  \(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\) is a quasi-isomorphism; equivalently the
2411	  homology presheaves of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) vanish away from degree \(0\) and
2412	  equal \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) in degree \(0\).
2413	
2414	  The contracting homotopy is built directly at the \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-module
2415	  (section) level as the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) map — the same combinatorial
2416	  content as \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy}\) /
2417	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy\_spec}\) of
2418	  Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}.
2419	\end{proof}

variable (C)

/-- The computation of the homology of single complexes, as a natural isomorphism
`single C c j ⋙ homologyFunctor C c j ≅ 𝟭 C`. -/
@[simps!]
noncomputable def homologyFunctorSingleIso [CategoryWithHomology C] :
    single C c j ⋙ homologyFunctor C c j ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  NatIso.ofComponents (fun A => (singleObjHomologySelfIso c j A))
    (fun f => singleObjHomologySelfIso_hom_naturality c j f)

end HomologicalComplex

open HomologicalComplex

lemma ChainComplex.exactAt_succ_single_obj (A : C) (n : ℕ) :
    ExactAt ((single₀ C).obj A) (n + 1) :=
  exactAt_single_obj _ _ _ _ (by simp)

lemma CochainComplex.exactAt_succ_single_obj (A : C) (n : ℕ) :
    ExactAt ((single₀ C).obj A) (n + 1) :=
  exactAt_single_obj _ _ _ _ (by simp)

rw [quasiIsoAt_iff, ShortComplex.quasiIso_iff]
  rfl

lemma quasiIsoAt_iff_exactAt (f : K ⟶ L) (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i]
    (hK : K.ExactAt i) :
    QuasiIsoAt f i ↔ L.ExactAt i := by
  simp only [quasiIsoAt_iff, ShortComplex.quasiIso_iff, exactAt_iff,
    ShortComplex.exact_iff_isZero_homology] at hK ⊢
  constructor
  · intro h
    exact IsZero.of_iso hK (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ h).symm
  · intro hL
    exact ⟨⟨0, IsZero.eq_of_src hK _ _, IsZero.eq_of_tgt hL _ _⟩⟩

lemma quasiIsoAt_iff_exactAt' (f : K ⟶ L) (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i]
    (hL : L.ExactAt i) :
    QuasiIsoAt f i ↔ K.ExactAt i := by
  simp only [quasiIsoAt_iff, ShortComplex.quasiIso_iff, exactAt_iff,
    ShortComplex.exact_iff_isZero_homology] at hL ⊢
  constructor
  · intro h
    exact IsZero.of_iso hL (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ h)
  · intro hK
    exact ⟨⟨0, IsZero.eq_of_src hK _ _, IsZero.eq_of_tgt hL _ _⟩⟩

lemma exactAt_iff_of_quasiIsoAt (f : K ⟶ L) (i : ι)
    [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i] [QuasiIsoAt f i] :
    K.ExactAt i ↔ L.ExactAt i :=
  ⟨fun hK => (quasiIsoAt_iff_exactAt f i hK).1 inferInstance,
    fun hL => (quasiIsoAt_iff_exactAt' f i hL).1 inferInstance⟩

instance (f : K ⟶ L) (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i] [hf : QuasiIsoAt f i] :
    IsIso (homologyMap f i) := by
  simpa only [quasiIsoAt_iff, ShortComplex.quasiIso_iff] using hf

/-- The isomorphism `K.homology i ≅ L.homology i` induced by a morphism `f : K ⟶ L` such
that `[QuasiIsoAt f i]` holds. -/
@[simps! hom]
noncomputable def isoOfQuasiIsoAt (f : K ⟶ L) (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i]
    [QuasiIsoAt f i] : K.homology i ≅ L.homology i :=
  asIso (homologyMap f i)

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma isoOfQuasiIsoAt_hom_inv_id (f : K ⟶ L) (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i]
    [QuasiIsoAt f i] :
    homologyMap f i ≫ (isoOfQuasiIsoAt f i).inv = 𝟙 _ :=
  (isoOfQuasiIsoAt f i).hom_inv_id

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma isoOfQuasiIsoAt_inv_hom_id (f : K ⟶ L) (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i]
    [QuasiIsoAt f i] :
    (isoOfQuasiIsoAt f i).inv ≫ homologyMap f i = 𝟙 _ :=
  (isoOfQuasiIsoAt f i).inv_hom_id

lemma CochainComplex.quasiIsoAt₀_iff {K L : CochainComplex C ℕ} (f : K ⟶ L)
    [K.HasHomology 0] [L.HasHomology 0] [(K.sc' 0 0 1).HasHomology] [(L.sc' 0 0 1).HasHomology] :
    QuasiIsoAt f 0 ↔
      ShortComplex.QuasiIso ((HomologicalComplex.shortComplexFunctor' C _ 0 0 1).map f) :=
  quasiIsoAt_iff' _ _ _ _ (by simp) (by simp)

lemma ChainComplex.quasiIsoAt₀_iff {K L : ChainComplex C ℕ} (f : K ⟶ L)
    [K.HasHomology 0] [L.HasHomology 0] [(K.sc' 1 0 0).HasHomology] [(L.sc' 1 0 0).HasHomology] :
    QuasiIsoAt f 0 ↔
      ShortComplex.QuasiIso ((HomologicalComplex.shortComplexFunctor' C _ 1 0 0).map f) :=
  quasiIsoAt_iff' _ _ _ _ (by simp) (by simp)

/-- A morphism of homological complexes `f : K ⟶ L` is a quasi-isomorphism when it
is so in every degree, i.e. when the induced maps `homologyMap f i : K.homology i ⟶ L.homology i`
are all isomorphisms (see `quasiIso_iff` and `quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap`). -/
class QuasiIso (f : K ⟶ L) [∀ i, K.HasHomology i] [∀ i, L.HasHomology i] : Prop where
  quasiIsoAt : ∀ i, QuasiIsoAt f i := by infer_instance

(K L : ChainComplex C ℕ) (φ : K ⟶ L) [K.HasHomology 0]

instance isIso_iCycles₀ : IsIso (K.iCycles 0) :=
  K.isIso_iCycles 0 0 (by simp) (by simp)

/-- The canonical isomorphism `K.cycles 0 ≅ K.X 0` for a chain complex `K`
indexed by `ℕ`. -/
noncomputable abbrev cycles₀Iso : K.cycles 0 ≅ K.X 0 :=
  K.iCyclesIso 0 0 (by simp) (by simp)

instance isIso_homologyι₀ :
    IsIso (K.homologyι 0) :=
  K.isIso_homologyι 0 _ rfl (by simp)

/-- The canonical isomorphism `K.homology 0 ≅ K.opcycles 0` for a chain complex `K`
indexed by `ℕ`. -/
noncomputable abbrev isoHomologyι₀ : K.homology 0 ≅ K.opcycles 0 :=
  K.isoHomologyι 0 _ rfl (by simp)

variable {K L}

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma isoHomologyι₀_inv_naturality [L.HasHomology 0] :
    K.isoHomologyι₀.inv ≫ HomologicalComplex.homologyMap φ 0 =
      HomologicalComplex.opcyclesMap φ 0 ≫ L.isoHomologyι₀.inv := by
  simp only [assoc, ← cancel_mono (L.homologyι 0),
    HomologicalComplex.homologyι_naturality, HomologicalComplex.isoHomologyι_inv_hom_id_assoc,
    HomologicalComplex.isoHomologyι_inv_hom_id, comp_id]

end ChainComplex

namespace CochainComplex

variable {C : Type*} [Category* C] [HasZeroMorphisms C]
  (K L : CochainComplex C ℕ) (φ : K ⟶ L) [K.HasHomology 0]

instance isIso_pOpcycles₀ : IsIso (K.pOpcycles 0) :=
  K.isIso_pOpcycles 0 0 (by simp) (by simp)

/-- The canonical isomorphism `K.X 0 ≅ K.opcycles 0` for a cochain complex `K`
indexed by `ℕ`. -/
noncomputable abbrev opcycles₀Iso : K.X 0 ≅ K.opcycles 0 :=
  K.pOpcyclesIso 0 0 (by simp) (by simp)

instance isIso_homologyπ₀ :
    IsIso (K.homologyπ 0) :=
  K.isIso_homologyπ _ 0 rfl (by simp)

/-- The canonical isomorphism `K.cycles 0 ≅ K.homology 0` for a cochain complex `K`
indexed by `ℕ`. -/
noncomputable abbrev isoHomologyπ₀ : K.cycles 0 ≅ K.homology 0 :=
  K.isoHomologyπ _ 0 rfl (by simp)

variable {K L}

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma isoHomologyπ₀_inv_naturality [L.HasHomology 0] :
    HomologicalComplex.homologyMap φ 0 ≫ L.isoHomologyπ₀.inv =
      K.isoHomologyπ₀.inv ≫ HomologicalComplex.cyclesMap φ 0 := by
  simp only [← cancel_epi (K.homologyπ 0), HomologicalComplex.homologyπ_naturality_assoc,
    HomologicalComplex.isoHomologyπ_hom_inv_id, comp_id,
    HomologicalComplex.isoHomologyπ_hom_inv_id_assoc]

end CochainComplex

namespace HomologicalComplex

variable {C ι : Type*} [Category* C] [Preadditive C] {c : ComplexShape ι}
  {K L : HomologicalComplex C c} {f g : K ⟶ L}

variable (φ ψ : K ⟶ L) (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]
lemma homologyMap_neg : homologyMap (-φ) i = -homologyMap φ i := by
  dsimp [homologyMap]
  rw [← ShortComplex.homologyMap_neg]
  rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]
lemma homologyMap_add : homologyMap (φ + ψ) i = homologyMap φ i + homologyMap ψ i := by
  dsimp [homologyMap]
  rw [← ShortComplex.homologyMap_add]
  rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]
lemma homologyMap_sub : homologyMap (φ - ψ) i = homologyMap φ i - homologyMap ψ i := by
  dsimp [homologyMap]
  rw [← ShortComplex.homologyMap_sub]

/-- **The augmentation chain map** `cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V ⟶ O_X(V)[0]`, whose degree-`0` component
is the codiagonal `cechEngineAug0`.  The chain-map condition is `cechEngineD_comp_aug`. -/
noncomputable def cechEngineComplexAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
    cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V ⟶ (ChainComplex.single₀ _).obj (coverSectionModule V) :=
  ((cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V).toSingle₀Equiv (coverSectionModule V)).symm
    ⟨cechEngineAug0 𝒰 V, by
      rw [show (cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V).d 1 0 = cechEngineD 𝒰 V 0 from ChainComplex.of_d _ _ _ 0]
      exact cechEngineD_comp_aug 𝒰 V⟩

end AlgebraicGeometry
input.new_string:
/-- **The augmentation chain map** `cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V ⟶ O_X(V)[0]`, whose degree-`0` component
is the codiagonal `cechEngineAug0`.  The chain-map condition is `cechEngineD_comp_aug`. -/
noncomputable def cechEngineComplexAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
    cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V ⟶ (ChainComplex.single₀ _).obj (coverSectionModule V) :=
  ((cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V).toSingle₀Equiv (coverSectionModule V)).symm
    ⟨cechEngineAug0 𝒰 V, by
      rw [show (cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V).d 1 0 = cechEngineD 𝒰 V 0 from ChainComplex.of_d _ _ _ 0]
      exact cechEngineD_comp_aug 𝒰 V⟩

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — degree-`0` splitting and engine quasi-isomorphism -/

/-- **Degree-`0` contracting identity of the augmented engine complex.** Fixing
`i_fix ∈ I₁(V)`, the prepend map `s := cechEnginePrepend 0`, the differential `d := cechEngineD 0`,
the augmentation `g := cechEngineAug0` and the section `r := ι_{(fun _ => i_fix)}` of `g` satisfy
`s ≫ d + g ≫ r = 𝟙`.  Together with `r ≫ g = 𝟙` this exhibits `g` as the cokernel of `d` (and the
augmented complex `C₁ → C₀ → O_X(V)` as exact at `C₀`), the degree-`0` input to the engine
quasi-isomorphism. -/
lemma cechEngineAug0_split (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) :
    cechEnginePrepend 𝒰 V i_fix 0 ≫ cechEngineD 𝒰 V 0
      + cechEngineAug0 𝒰 V ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι
          (fun _ : Fin 1 → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} => coverSectionModule V)
          (fun _ => i_fix)
      = 𝟙 (cechEngineX 𝒰 V 0) := by
  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
  intro σ
  rw [Preadditive.comp_add, Category.comp_id]
  rw [← Category.assoc, cechEnginePrepend_ι, cechEngineD_ι]
  rw [← Category.assoc, cechEngineAug0_ι, Category.id_comp]
  rw [Fin.sum_univ_two]
  have e0 : (Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin 2 → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i})
      ∘ (0 : Fin 2).succAbove = σ := by funext k; simp
  have e1 : (Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin 2 → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i})
      ∘ (1 : Fin 2).succAbove = (fun _ => i_fix) := by
    funext k; fin_cases k; simp
  rw [e0, e1]
  simp only [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
  abel

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- The degree-`0` component of the engine augmentation chain map is the codiagonal
`cechEngineAug0`. -/
lemma cechEngineComplexAug_f_zero (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
    (cechEngineComplexAug 𝒰 V).f 0 = cechEngineAug0 𝒰 V := by
  rw [cechEngineComplexAug, ChainComplex.toSingle₀Equiv_symm_apply_f_zero]

/-- **The engine augmentation is a quasi-isomorphism** (nonempty case).  When `I₁(V)` is nonempty
(witnessed by `i_fix`), `cechEngineComplexAug 𝒰 V : cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V ⟶ O_X(V)[0]` is a
quasi-isomorphism: positive-degree exactness comes from the contracting homotopy
(`cechEngineComplex_exactAt`), and degree `0` from the splitting `cechEngineAug0_split` exhibiting
`cechEngineAug0` as the cokernel of the degree-`0` differential. -/
lemma cechEngineComplexAug_quasiIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) :
    QuasiIso (cechEngineComplexAug 𝒰 V) := by
  rw [quasiIso_iff]
  rintro (_ | n)
  · rw [ChainComplex.quasiIsoAt₀_iff]
    sorry
  · rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_exactAt' _ _ (ChainComplex.exactAt_succ_single_obj _ n)]
    exact cechEngineComplex_exactAt 𝒰 V i_fix n

end AlgebraicGeometry

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma quasiIso_iff_isIso_liftCycles (φ : S₁ ⟶ S₂)
    (hf₁ : S₁.f = 0) (hg₁ : S₁.g = 0) (hf₂ : S₂.f = 0) :
    QuasiIso φ ↔ IsIso (S₂.liftCycles φ.τ₂ (by rw [φ.comm₂₃, hg₁, zero_comp])) := by
  let H : LeftHomologyMapData φ (LeftHomologyData.ofZeros S₁ hf₁ hg₁)
      (LeftHomologyData.ofIsLimitKernelFork S₂ hf₂ _ S₂.cyclesIsKernel) :=
    { φK := S₂.liftCycles φ.τ₂ (by rw [φ.comm₂₃, hg₁, zero_comp])
      φH := S₂.liftCycles φ.τ₂ (by rw [φ.comm₂₃, hg₁, zero_comp]) }
  exact H.quasiIso_iff

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma quasiIso_iff_isIso_descOpcycles (φ : S₁ ⟶ S₂)
    (hg₁ : S₁.g = 0) (hf₂ : S₂.f = 0) (hg₂ : S₂.g = 0) :
    QuasiIso φ ↔ IsIso (S₁.descOpcycles φ.τ₂ (by rw [← φ.comm₁₂, hf₂, comp_zero])) := by
  let H : RightHomologyMapData φ
      (RightHomologyData.ofIsColimitCokernelCofork S₁ hg₁ _ S₁.opcyclesIsCokernel)
        (RightHomologyData.ofZeros S₂ hf₂ hg₂) :=
    { φQ := S₁.descOpcycles φ.τ₂ (by rw [← φ.comm₁₂, hf₂, comp_zero])
      φH := S₁.descOpcycles φ.τ₂ (by rw [← φ.comm₁₂, hf₂, comp_zero]) }
  exact H.quasiIso_iff

end ShortComplex

end CategoryTheory

  map f :=
    { τ₁ := f.f i
      τ₂ := f.f j
      τ₃ := f.f k }

app M := M.map f

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The obvious free presheaf of modules of rank `1`. -/
noncomputable def unit : PresheafOfModules R where
  obj X := ModuleCat.of _ (R.obj X)
  -- TODO: after https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/pull/19511 we need to hint `(Y := ...)`.
  -- This suggests `restrictScalars` needs to be redesigned.
  map {X Y} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
      (Y := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (ModuleCat.of (R.obj Y) (R.obj Y)))
    { toFun := fun x ↦ R.map f x
      map_add' := by simp
      map_smul' := by cat_disch }

lemma unit_map_one {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) : (unit R).map f (1 : R.obj X) = (1 : R.obj Y) :=
  (R.map f).hom.map_one

variable {R}

/-- The type of sections of a presheaf of modules. -/
def sections (M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R) : Type _ := (M.presheaf ⋙ forget _).sections

/-- Given a presheaf of modules `M`, `s : M.sections` and `X : Cᵒᵖ`, this is the induced

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — free Čech quasi-isomorphism (nonempty case + assembly) -/

/-- **Degree-`0` augmentation comm-square (engine identification).** Evaluating the free Čech
augmentation `cechFreeAug` at `V` and the engine codiagonal `cechEngineAug0` agree under the
degree-`0` object identification `cechFreeEvalEngine_X`.  This is the degree-`0` analogue of
`cechFreeEvalEngine_comm` and the key bridge for the nonempty quasi-isomorphism: it identifies the
evaluated free augmentation with the engine augmentation. -/
private lemma cechFreeAug_eval_eq (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map (cechFreeAug 𝒰)
      = (cechFreeEvalEngine_X 𝒰 V 0).hom ≫ cechEngineAug0 𝒰 V := by
  refine (cancel_epi (cechFreeEval_X 𝒰 (Opposite.op V) 0).inv).mp ?_
  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
  intro σ
  by_cases hσ : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ
  · slice_rhs 1 3 => rw [cechFreeEvalEngine_X_inv_hom_ι 𝒰 V 0 σ hσ]
    slice_rhs 2 3 => rw [cechEngineAug0_ι]
    rw [Category.comp_id]
    slice_lhs 1 2 => rw [cechFreeEval_X_ι_inv 𝒰 V 0 σ]
    rw [← Functor.map_comp]
    have hsd : Limits.Sigma.ι
          (fun σ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) σ ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰
        = freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) := by
      rw [cechFreeAug, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
    rw [hsd, PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map, ← freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le_hom_eq_aug hσ]
  · exact (freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le hσ).eq_of_src _ _

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- The engine codiagonal `cechEngineAug0` is a (split) epimorphism in the nonempty case: the
constant-`i_fix` injection is a section. -/
private lemma epi_cechEngineAug0 (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) : Epi (cechEngineAug0 𝒰 V) := by
  have : IsSplitEpi (cechEngineAug0 𝒰 V) :=
    ⟨⟨⟨Limits.Sigma.ι _ (fun _ => i_fix), cechEngineAug0_ι 𝒰 V (fun _ => i_fix)⟩⟩⟩
  infer_instance

/-- **The cover structure presheaf evaluates to `O_X(V)` over an open meeting a cover member.**
When `I₁(V) ≠ ∅` (witnessed by `i_fix`), `(eval V).obj O_𝒰 ≅ O_X(V)`.  The augmentation
`cechFreeAug` evaluates (by `cechFreeAug_eval_eq`) to the engine codiagonal up to the degree-`0`
object iso, hence is an epimorphism; since `O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` and evaluation is exact, the
evaluated image inclusion `(eval V)(image.ι)` is mono and epi, hence iso. -/
noncomputable def coverStructurePresheafEval_iso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) :
    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
        (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰) ≅ coverSectionModule V := by
  haveI : Epi (cechEngineAug0 𝒰 V) := epi_cechEngineAug0 𝒰 V i_fix
  haveI hEpiAug : Epi ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
      (cechFreeAug 𝒰)) := by
    rw [cechFreeAug_eval_eq]; infer_instance
  haveI : Epi ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
      (Limits.image.ι (cechFreeAug 𝒰))) := by
    refine epi_of_epi_fac (f := (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj
      (Opposite.op V)).map (Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰))) ?_
    rw [← Functor.map_comp, Limits.image.fac]
  haveI : Mono ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
      (Limits.image.ι (cechFreeAug 𝒰))) := inferInstance
  haveI : IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
      (Limits.image.ι (cechFreeAug 𝒰))) := isIso_of_mono_of_epi _
  exact asIso ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
    (Limits.image.ι (cechFreeAug 𝒰)))

end AlgebraicGeometry

lemma quasiIso_of_arrow_mk_iso (φ : K ⟶ L) (φ' : K' ⟶ L') (e : Arrow.mk φ ≅ Arrow.mk φ')
    [∀ i, K.HasHomology i] [∀ i, L.HasHomology i]
    [∀ i, K'.HasHomology i] [∀ i, L'.HasHomology i]
    [hφ : QuasiIso φ] : QuasiIso φ' := by
  simpa only [← quasiIso_iff_of_arrow_mk_iso φ φ' e]

lemma quasiIso_of_retractArrow {f : K ⟶ L} {f' : K' ⟶ L'}
    (h : RetractArrow f f') [∀ i, K.HasHomology i] [∀ i, L.HasHomology i]
    [∀ i, K'.HasHomology i] [∀ i, L'.HasHomology i] [QuasiIso f'] :
    QuasiIso f where
  quasiIsoAt i := quasiIsoAt_of_retract h i

=== isoMk sig ===
and a proof that the square commutes. -/
@[simps!]
def isoMk {f g : Arrow T} (l : f.left ≅ g.left) (r : f.right ≅ g.right)
    (h : l.hom ≫ g.hom = f.hom ≫ r.hom := by cat_disch) : f ≅ g :=
  Comma.isoMk l r h

/-- A variant of `Arrow.isoMk` that creates an iso between two `Arrow.mk`s with a better type
signature. -/
abbrev isoMk' {W X Y Z : T} (f : W ⟶ X) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (e₁ : W ≅ Y) (e₂ : X ≅ Z)
    (h : e₁.hom ≫ g = f ≫ e₂.hom := by cat_disch) : Arrow.mk f ≅ Arrow.mk g :=

/-- **Evaluated free Čech quasi-isomorphism, nonempty case** (`lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty`).

`cechFreeComplexAug` is a quasi-isomorphism.  Transferred across `cechFreeEvalEngineIso` from the
engine quasi-isomorphism `cechEngineComplexAug_quasiIso`, using the degree-`0` augmentation
identification `cechFreeAug_eval_eq` and the geometric identification
`coverStructurePresheafEval_iso`. -/
lemma cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) :
    QuasiIso (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj
      (Opposite.op V)).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.down ℕ)).map (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰)) := by
  haveI := cechEngineComplexAug_quasiIso 𝒰 V i_fix
  set F := PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V) with hF
  set rightIso := (HomologicalComplex.singleMapHomologicalComplex F (ComplexShape.down ℕ) 0).app
      (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰) ≪≫
      (ChainComplex.single₀ _).mapIso (coverStructurePresheafEval_iso 𝒰 V i_fix) with hRight
  have hcomm : (cechFreeEvalEngineIso 𝒰 V).hom ≫ cechEngineComplexAug 𝒰 V
      = (F.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.down ℕ)).map (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰) ≫ rightIso.hom := by
    apply (ChainComplex.toSingle₀Equiv _ _).injective
    apply Subtype.ext
    rw [ChainComplex.toSingle₀Equiv_apply_coe, ChainComplex.toSingle₀Equiv_apply_coe,
      HomologicalComplex.comp_f, HomologicalComplex.comp_f]
    rw [cechEngineComplexAug_f_zero]
    rw [HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents_hom_f]
    rw [hRight, Iso.trans_hom, HomologicalComplex.comp_f,
      HomologicalComplex.singleMapHomologicalComplex_hom_app_self,
      ChainComplex.single₀_map_f_zero, coverStructurePresheafEval_iso_hom]
    rw [Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_map_f, cechFreeComplexAug_f_zero]
    -- goal: engineX0.hom ≫ aug0 = F.map factorThruImage ≫ (F.map singleObjXSelf.hom ≫ singleObjXSelf.inv ≫ F.map image.ι)
    sorry
  exact quasiIso_of_arrow_mk_iso (cechEngineComplexAug 𝒰 V) _
    (Arrow.isoMk' _ _ (cechFreeEvalEngineIso 𝒰 V) rightIso hcomm).symm

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- **The free Čech complex resolves the cover structure presheaf** (`lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`).
The augmentation chain map `cechFreeComplexAug : K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism.  By the
objectwise reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation`, it suffices to check this after evaluation at every
open `V`; the empty case (`cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty`) and the nonempty case
(`cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty`) cover both possibilities. -/
theorem cechFreeComplex_quasiIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
    QuasiIso (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰) := by
  apply quasiIso_of_evaluation
  intro V
  obtain ⟨W, rfl⟩ : ∃ W, V = Opposite.op W := ⟨V.unop, rfl⟩
  by_cases h : ∃ i, W ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i
  · obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := h
    exact cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty 𝒰 W ⟨i, hi⟩
  · push_neg at h
    exact cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty 𝒰 W h

end AlgebraicGeometry

  - `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty` (1401) — `lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty`.
  - `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` (1436) — **`lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`, THE named target**.
- Declarations blocked: NONE.

## Proof architecture (what landed)
The objective's planned route worked end-to-end (Route B, NOT homotopy repackaging):

1. **Engine resolution** `cechEngineComplexAug_quasiIso` (the self-contained core):
   - positive degrees via `quasiIsoAt_iff_exactAt'` + `ChainComplex.exactAt_succ_single_obj` +
     the already-built `cechEngineComplex_exactAt`;
   - degree 0 via `ChainComplex.quasiIsoAt₀_iff` + `ShortComplex.quasiIso_iff_isIso_descOpcycles`,
     where `descOpcycles cechEngineAug0` is shown iso by exhibiting an explicit inverse
     `ι_{const i_fix} ≫ pOpcycles`, using the splitting `cechEngineAug0_split` (`s≫d + g≫r = 𝟙`)
     and `S.f_pOpcycles`.

2. **Degree-0 bridge** `cechFreeAug_eval_eq`: same `Sigma.hom_ext` + `by_cases V ≤ U_σ` structure as
   `cechFreeEvalEngine_comm`, using `reassoc_of%` of `cechFreeEval_X_ι_inv` /
   `cechFreeEvalEngine_X_inv_hom_ι`, `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le_hom_eq_aug`, and `cechEngineAug0_ι`.

3. **Geometric identification** `coverStructurePresheafEval_iso`: `O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)`,
   evaluation is exact, so `(eval)(image.ι)` is mono (eval preserves finite limits ⇒
   `preservesMonomorphisms`) and epi (from `(eval)(cechFreeAug)` epi via `cechFreeAug_eval_eq` =
   iso ≫ split-epi `cechEngineAug0`); mono+epi ⇒ iso (`isIso_of_mono_of_epi`, `Balanced (ModuleCat _)`).

4. **Nonempty case** `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty`: arrow-iso transfer
   `quasiIso_of_arrow_mk_iso` from `cechEngineComplexAug` along
   `Arrow.isoMk' _ _ (cechFreeEvalEngineIso 𝒰 V) rightIso hcomm`, where
   `rightIso = singleMapHomologicalComplex ≪≫ single₀.mapIso coverStructurePresheafEval_iso`.
   The chain-level comm-square `hcomm` reduces (via `ChainComplex.toSingle₀Equiv.injective` +
   `Subtype.ext`) to a degree-0 identity that is exactly `cechFreeAug_eval_eq` modulo
   `singleObjXSelf` `eqToHom` bookkeeping (cleared by `erw [eqToHom_refl]`).

5. **Glue** `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`: `quasiIso_of_evaluation` per open `V`, `by_cases ∃ i, V ≤ U_i`,
   dispatching to `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty` (already in file) / `_of_nonempty`.

## Key Lean lessons (for next iters / reviewer)
- **`erw` is essential here**: many ShortComplex/`single` rewrites fail under `rw` due to
  defeq-but-not-syntactic mismatches — notably `(eval).obj unit ≡ coverSectionModule V`,
  `Fin (0+1) ≡ Fin 1`, `(e.app X).hom ≡ e.hom.app X`, and the `singleObjXSelf` `eqToHom` bridge
  `F((single₀ A).X 0) ≡ F A`. Use `erw` / `@asIso _ _ _ _ _ inst` (explicit instance) / `exact …`
  (defeq) to get past these.
- `simp` leaves `(0 : A ⟶ B) = 0` open on `K.d i j` non-adjacent goals; use bare `rfl` instead
  (the `ChainComplex.of` differential reduces definitionally).
- `reassoc_of% lemma` is the clean way to apply a 3-term composition lemma inside a longer chain.
